name: Alain Dermarkar
bio: Alain serves as the U.S. Head of Private Equity and U.S. Co-Head of M&A. With a robust practice that focuses on U.S. and international mergers and acquisitions (M&A) and other strategic transactions, including minority investments and joint ventures, Alain's extensive experience in M&A and PE has positioned him as a key figure in the industry. Alain's private capital and strategic clients operate and invest in a diverse range of industries including: technology; manufacturing; aerospace/defense; environmental; construction; business services; healthcare; consumer products; energy; and real estate. Alain has an impressive track record, having completed transactions exceeding US275 billion for public and private companies across the U.S., Europe, and Asia, as well as for private equity firms. His expertise in handling high-value transactions underscores his leadership in the M&A and PE sectors. In addition to his transactional work, Alain provides comprehensive advisory services, including: Capital Raising Transactions: Guiding clients through the complexities of raising capital to support growth and strategic initiatives; Corporate Governance: Advising on best practices and regulatory compliance to ensure robust governance frameworks; Takeover Preparedness: Preparing clients for potential takeover scenarios to safeguard their interests; and SPAC Transactions: Offering specialized counsel on Special Purpose Acquisition Company (SPAC) related transactions.
jaide: Alain specializes in U.S. and international mergers and acquisitions (M&A) and private equity transactions across various industries.
specialties: Capital Markets, Compliance/Regulatory, M&A, Private Equity, Joint Ventures, Energy, Asia

name: Joshua Ebersole
bio: Josh focuses his practice on representing companies and financial institutions in complex securities class actions, regulatory matters, and commercial litigation in federal and state courts. He has also represented companies in regulatory and internal investigations, and provides disclosure advice to public companies on litigation risk-related issues.
specialties: Investigations, Banking, Commercial, Securities, Class Action

name: Jeffrey Hoschander
bio: Jeff frequently represents corporations, directors, and officers in high-stakes litigation and investigations. Jeff broadly focuses on complex commercial, life sciences, securities, and shareholder derivative litigation, as well as regulatory enforcement matters and board committee representations related to corporate governance. With a distinct perspective attributable to having earned a medical degree, Jeff often advises and represents clients in the pharmaceutical, biotech, and medical device industries on litigation, regulatory, and transaction-related matters.
specialties: Investigations, Pharmaceuticals, Commercial, Securities

name: Adele Lee
bio: Adele Lee is a partner at A.Y. Strauss. She represents clients in connection with commercial real estate transactions, corporate finance and distressed debt transactions. Adele began her career as a real estate finance associate at Dechert LLP, where she represented major institutional lenders in multi-state real estate debt financing. She worked extensively in originating mortgage and mezzanine loans for the development and financing of shopping centers, office complexes and other commercial properties. Most recently, Ms. Lee was an associate in the debt trading group of Richards, Kibbe & Orbe LLP. There, she represented investment banks, hedge funds and other financial institutions in connection with the purchase and sale of distressed assets, including, among other things, domestic and international syndicated bank loans, and claims in bankruptcy and insolvency proceedings. Adele has also undertaken numerous pro bono engagements. She assisted hurricane victims to file succession documents in order to establish heirship to claim federal aid to repair damaged homes owned by deceased relatives. In addition, she advised a low-income Housing Development Fund Corporation (HDFC) in New York on the closing of a loan from a credit union, acting as liaison between the HDFC, the lender and the Urban Homestead Assistance Board.
specialties: Finance, Affordable Housing, Distressed Debt

name: David Salhanick
bio: David S. Salhanick is a partner at A.Y. Strauss. He focuses his practice on commercial and real estate litigation. David has guided some of New York's best known real estate developers, property managers, and business owners through complex, high-stakes legal disputes. As an experienced and dynamic litigator, David's strong research, writing, and courtroom skills are matched by a careful attention to detail. His solutions-driven approach to litigation has allowed him to consistently achieve favorable results for his clients before, during, and after trial. David is frequently called upon to handle appeals and motions in various appellate courts. Outside of the litigation context, David is a trusted source of general counsel and practical legal guidance for his clients.
specialties: Litigation, Appellate, Commercial, Real Estate

name: Peter Hawkins
bio: Pete Hawkins focuses his practice on both commercial litigation and intellectual property matters. He has tried jury cases to verdict in both federal and state court, and has experience in all aspects of trial and appellate practice. Pete has argued numerous pre- and post-trial motions in venues throughout the United States.Pete's intellectual property practice encompasses a wide variety of technologies, including medical devices, oil and gas refining, semiconductor memory, telecommunications, diesel engines, and wireless devices. Pete has experience litigating patent, trademark, and trade secret cases, as well as providing patent validity and infringement analyses for both opinion and due diligence matters.In his spare time, Pete does his best to keep up with his three young children. He enjoys exploring the numerous restaurants and craft breweries Chicago has to offer. Because of that, Pete also attempts to enjoy running.
specialties: Litigation, CR/TM Litigation, Trademark, Trade Secrets, Patent Litigation, Commercial

name: Lyndey Bryant
bio: Lyndey Bryant is a skilled and experienced commercial litigator, who has represented large and small businesses, officers, and directors in complex civil litigation, arbitration, appeals, as well as administrative proceedings, across a variety of practice areas. With over ten years of experience, she has developed a keen understanding of the legal issues that can arise in business settings, and is able to provide her clients with strategic and effective solutions to their legal disputes. Her professional achievements and service to the Bar have been honored numerous times. Lyndey has experience with a wide range of business issues arising in litigation. In particular, she has frequently counseled companies and individuals regarding enforcement and negotiation of complex contract provisions, including employment, non-compete, confidentiality, and trade secret agreements. In addition, Lyndey also has experience representing fiduciaries and beneficiaries in disputes related to wealth transfer, such as estate, trust, real estate, and closely-held business disputes, and the exercise of fiduciary duties. In these matters, Lyndey has counseled clients regarding issues of enforcement and modification of estate and trust provisions.
specialties: Appellate, Commercial, Arbitration

name: Ilya Leyvi
bio: Ilya Leyvi chairs Adler & Stachenfeld's Real Estate Finance Practice, where he facilitates some of the largest and most complex commercial real estate debt deals in the U.S. and internationally. Recognized as an astute and approachable counsel, Mr. Leyvi represents lenders and borrowers with bridge loans, construction loans, mezzanine loans, commercial mortgage-backed securities loans, and preferred equity investments involving industrial, hotel, multi-family, self-storage, and specialty properties. As a practice leader, he is responsible for structuring and executing complicated commercial real estate debt platforms and arrangements for lenders, as well as borrowers, and identifying growth opportunities for his clients. Mr. Leyvi excels in this regard in part through his collaborative approach by working closely with his clients and their deal counterparts. He ensures that all parties walk away knowing their positions are heard and carefully considered – ultimately enabling him to make highly intricate structured transactions move forward seamlessly. Mr. Leyvi joined A&S in 2013. He is a magna cum laude graduate of Cornell Law School and earned his B.B.A. summa cum laude from CUNY Baruch College – Zicklin School of Business. Mr. Leyvi is admitted to practice in New York.
jaide: Ilya specializes in facilitating large and complex commercial real estate debt deals in the U.S. and internationally.
specialties: Finance, Construction, Hotels & Resorts

name: Danielle Dufault
bio: Danielle is a member of the firm's Business & Corporate, Real Estate, Land Use, Hospitality and Gaming Law Groups. Danielle has a wide range of experience in assisting clients with various corporate and business law matters, including commercial and residential real estate transactions and land use projects, stock and asset sales and acquisitions, commercial financing, and corporate governance. After retiring from a distinguished career as a professional figure skating coach, Danielle resumed her higher education goals and graduated from the University of Rhode Island and Roger Williams University School of Law. Danielle brings the same passion, dedication and attention to detail that made her figure skating accomplishments possible to her legal practice at AP&S.
specialties: Land Use, P&S, M&A, Finance, Hospitality

name: Dustin Hillsley
bio: Dustin Hillsley acts as lead counsel in a wide variety of complex commercial litigation matters, with emphasis on private equity, commercial real estate, M&A, bet-the-company, shareholder and partnership matters (including business divorce), and other forms of business litigation. Serving as outside counsel to owners and business leaders for companies across the country, he regularly provides advice regarding statutory and contractual rights and obligations. Dustin leverages his deep knowledge of the law to craft strategic, practical, and business-driven solutions for his clients. He began his practice in Delaware's Court of Chancery and is co-author of the Bloomberg BNA Corporate Practice Series Portfolio, Derivative Lawsuits. Prior to joining Akerman, Dustin practiced at a law firm in Wilmington, Delaware, specializing in bet-the-company and other high-stakes corporate litigation matters and an Am Law 100 international law firm in Washington, D.C.
jaide: Dustin specializes in complex commercial litigation matters, particularly in private equity, commercial real estate, M&A, shareholder and partnership disputes, and other business litigation.
specialties: Commercial, Real Estate, Family Law

name: Daniel Silva
bio: Daniel Andres Silva has a wide-ranging corporate practice advising private equity funds, public and private companies, and start-ups in a variety of complex corporate transactions and general corporate matters, including mergers, acquisitions, divestitures, private equity investments, joint ventures, securities transactions, restructurings, fund formations, corporate governance, and private placements. Daniel has represented private equity sponsors and their portfolio companies in domestic and cross-border transactions, licensed pari-mutuel facility and gaming licenses in corporate transactions, and multistate cannabis operators in the acquisition of cannabis licensees in multiple jurisdictions.
specialties: Restructuring, Cross-Border, M&A, Joint Ventures, Private Equity, Securities, Fund Formation, Cannabis

name: Ryan Cox
firm: Akin, Gump, Strauss, Hauer & Feld, LLP
bio: Head of Akin's preferred equity & hybrid capital practice and a member of the corporate, special situations and capital solutions groups. Significant experience providing advice on bespoke capital solutions and structured equity financings. Represents leading asset managers and other capital providers, including credit, opportunities, private equity and venture funds, as well public and private issuers. Advises clients in connection with general corporate and capital markets matters, including Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) reporting requirements for public companies. Ryan is the head of Akin's preferred equity & hybrid capital practice and member of the corporate, special situations and capital solutions groups. His practice encompasses investments and transactions across the capital structure, including advising on private and public structured equity and complex corporate transactions. From convertible and non-convertible preferred equity to equity kickers and other convertible securities, Ryan partners with clients to deliver bespoke capital solutions spanning debt, equity and hybrid financings. His clients include a broad array of asset managers and key market participants, including credit, opportunities, private equity, growth and venture funds, family offices and other investors, as well as issuers. Ryan also advises on distressed and other special situations, recapitalizations and restructurings, mergers & acquisitions (M&A) and traditional corporate matters, including public company reporting. He has extensive capital markets experience, including initial and secondary offerings of common, preferred and convertible securities, Rule 144A offerings, rights offerings and shelf registrations. With experience across sectors, Ryan advises clients on financings and matters impacting investment management & private equity, energy & infrastructure, software and technology, among other industries.
jaide: Ryan specializes in providing advice on bespoke capital solutions and structured equity financings, representing leading asset managers and other capital providers.
specialties: Securities, Energy, Finance, M&A, Private Equity, Public Company, Investment Management, Restructuring, Capital Markets

name: Ryan Katz
firm: Akin, Gump, Strauss, Hauer & Feld, LLP
bio: Handles domestic and international corporate transactions. Also advises on corporate governance and general corporate matters. Has specific experience with pharmaceutical and biopharmaceutical clients. Ryan focuses his practice on corporate transactions for private equity and hedge fund clients, including mergers and acquisitions, advising on portfolio company investments and guiding fund sponsors and managers in forming new funds. He also advises clients on a variety of financial transactions involving their investment activities, including acquisitions and dispositions of portfolio investments and corporate financing transactions. Ryan has market-leading experience in royalty-rights transactions, particularly in the pharmaceutical and biopharmaceutical sectors. In addition, Ryan has worked with many of the leading investors in investment fund seeding transactions. Ryan handles domestic and international corporate transactions including: Public and private mergers and acquisitions Private equity and hedge fund investments Venture capital transactions Debt financings Joint ventures and strategic alliances Royalty monetization transactions Investment fund seeding transactions.
specialties: M&A, Private Equity, Restructuring, Joint Ventures, Hedge Funds

name: Jacqueline Yecies
firm: Akin, Gump, Strauss, Hauer & Feld, LLP
bio: Represents investment funds and private equity firm clients in civil litigation, including both manager and investor side disputes; portfolio company litigation; compliance advice; and regulatory investigations and enforcement proceedings. Advises investment managers and investors regarding GP/LP disputes and GP removals, disputes between principals and capital withdrawal and redemption issues; and counsels corporations and directors on securities, fiduciary and conflicts issues. Served as lead counsel on multibillion-dollar litigations, achieved multiple trial victories in federal and bankruptcy courts, and ran successful arbitrations and mediations. Jackie leads the firm's funds litigation initiative, a cross-practice group of lawyers representing clients from fund formation to maturity. The team provides investment funds, including hedge, sovereign wealth, and university and research endowment funds, as well as private equity firm clients, with compliance advice, representation in civil litigation and trials and guidance through regulatory investigations and proceedings. Jackie regularly represents institutional clients, including investment funds, private equity firms and international banks, as well as individual clients, in U.S. and cross-border investigations. On behalf of her clients, Jackie has presented to various U.S. civil and criminal regulatory agencies, including the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Department of Justice (DOJ). She also represents clients in investigations by Asian and European banking and commercial regulators. Jackie consistently manages key client relationships on multibillion-dollar litigations in state and federal courts in New York, Delaware and other domestic and international jurisdictions. She has led multiple trial teams to victory, including in federal court in the Southern District of New York (S.D.N.Y.) and in U.S. Bankruptcy Court in Delaware. Jackie has also led successful arbitrations and mediations before the American Arbitration Association (AAA) and Judicial Arbitration and Mediation Services (JAMS). Jackie represents clients in complex litigation related to: Limited partner/general partner disputes and general partner removals Disputes among fund principals Limited partner advisory committee (LPAC) representations Conflicts of interest and fiduciary issues Portfolio company investments Activist investments Shareholder class actions and derivative suits Federal securities actions, including Section 16(b) "short-swing profits" suits, as well as Section 10(b) and Section 11 claims Investment disputes in the cryptocurrency, blockchain and digital asset space Internal and regulatory investigations and enforcement issues
specialties: Class Action, Investigations, Securities, Commercial, Arbitration, Criminal, Banking, Bankruptcy

name: Caroline Chase
bio: Caroline practices land use law, with an emphasis on representing real estate developers in the entitlement of mixed-use, residential, commercial, industrial, biotechnology, and institutional development projects. She has excelled at helping clients navigate the complex entitlement processes throughout the state, including the San Francisco Bay Area. In particular, Caroline has extensive experience with utilizing state housing laws to facilitate the expedited approval of mixed-income multifamily housing development projects. Caroline has been practicing land use law almost two decades and has a unique multidisciplinary background including CEQA consulting, urban planning, zoning enforcement, and architecture. Her involvement and passion for her practice are also evident outside of the firm. Caroline is a long-standing member of the San Francisco Planning and Urban Research Association (SPUR) Housing Policy Board and served on the Housing Action Coalition (HAC) Board for over a decade. She is also an active CREW and Urban Land Institute (ULI) member. Accolades Named to Bisnow's NorCal Women Leading Real Estate (2022)
jaide: Caroline specializes in land use law, particularly representing real estate developers in the entitlement of various development projects.
specialties: Development, Land Use, Zoning

name: Amanda Donson
bio: Recognized for her superior client service and attention to detail, Amanda enjoys facilitating complex real estate transactions on behalf of her clients and closing challenging deals. Amanda represents a wide variety of clients including developers, homebuilders, private investors, institutional investors, and global technology companies in their acquisition, disposition, development and leasing of industrial, retail, office, and multifamily assets throughout the United States. From the early stages of drafting letters of intent to negotiating purchase agreements, analyzing due diligence, and managing the close of escrow, Amanda is a trusted advisor working collaboratively with her clients to achieve successful outcomes. With respect to her leasing work, she drafts and negotiates industrial, retail, and office leases, including build-to-suit leases, on behalf of landlords and tenants. In addition, Amanda drafts and negotiates CC&Rs, easement agreements, and development agreements in connection with her ground-up development work of shopping centers and residential subdivisions. While in law school, Amanda was a repeat recipient of the prestigious CALI Excellence for the Future Award for legal research, writing, and advocacy and honored for her commitment to serving the community through her pro bono legal work. She serves on the firm's Community Outreach and Pro Bono Committees, and has recently represented a non-profit in the acquisition of a commercial building to serve as their new headquarters. Amanda also serves as Co-Chair of the Allen Matkins Diversity & Inclusion committee. Accolades Named to The Best Lawyers in America "Ones to Watch" list (2025 - 2026) Recipient of the CREW Orange County SPIRE Awards in the category of Emerging Leader (2021)
specialties: Leasing, P&S, Development, Landlord/Tenant

name: Elizabeth Brown
bio: An experienced class action and health care litigator, Liz focuses on stellar client service and creative solutions for companies facing their most challenging legal issues. She approaches every case with an eye toward just and efficient resolution – whether that is achieved through motion practice, alternative dispute resolution, settlement, or trial. Liz Broadway Brown co-chairs Alston & Bird's Health Care Litigation Practice Team and has significant experience defending Fortune 500 companies in multiple industries in complex and high-profile litigation nationwide. Liz has represented clients before federal and state trial and appellate courts and arbitration panels across the country in cases involving health care, privacy, ERISA, antitrust, RICO, and numerous state consumer protection and deceptive trade practices act claims. Liz is also member of the firm's Class Action & MDL Practice Team and The Best Lawyers in America recognizes her as a leading class action and commercial litigation attorney. Liz was named to the Daily Report's "On the Rise" class of 2020—an elite group of Georgia's most promising lawyers under the age of 40—and to the Atlanta Business Chronicle's and The National Black Lawyers' "Top 40 Under 40" lists. She is also recognized as a Super Lawyer by Atlanta Magazine and is a member of the Leadership Atlanta Class of 2024. Liz was appointed to a three-year term on the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Georgia's Magistrate Judge Merit Selection Panel in 2022. In that role, she assists the district judges in the selection, appointment, and reappointment of magistrate judges in the District.
specialties: Commercial, Class Action, Unfair Competition, RICO, Health Care, Appellate, Data Privacy, Professional Liability

name: Tara Castillo
bio: Tara is a Chambers-ranked finance attorney who represents sponsors, originators, issuers, borrowers, lenders, underwriters, and other market participants in structured finance and securitization transactions across a broad spectrum of asset classes. She is go-to counsel for new market entrants in navigating operational and legal aspects of inaugural warehouse finance and securitization programs. Tara Eliza Castillo co-leads the firm's Finance Group. Tara represents clients in complex structured finance transactions, including warehouse facilities, securitizations, and other asset-backed transactions, in the U.S. and abroad. Financing and acquisitions of auto loans and leases, consumer loans, credit card receivables, elective medical health receivables, home improvement loans, lease-to-own receivables, merchant cash advances, solar loans, and timeshare receivables comprise significant portions of her practice. Tara advises fintech platforms with bank partnerships and forward flow arrangements. Tara's expertise also includes series trusts and titling trusts for the financing of traditional revolving assets, as well as non-revolving assets. Tara is a recognized industry speaker and has lectured at the Universidad Diego Portales and Universidad de Chile law schools in Santiago, Chile. She is part of the PLI's faculty for the annual "New Developments in Securitization" seminar. Tara is also the author of the "Securitization Readiness Considerations for Consumer Finance Companies," "Auto Loan Securitizations," and "Timeshare Loan Securitizations" chapters in the Securitization: Legal and Regulatory Issues treatise. Tara is a leading securitization lawyer in Chambers Global and Chambers USA (Capital Markets: Securitisation), IFLR1000 (Capital Markets: Structured Finance and Securitisation) as "highly regarded" and Women Leaders, and The Best Lawyers in America in securitization and structured finance law (2019–2023). Tara is counsel to the Structured Finance Association and serves on the cabinet of the Women in Securitization.
jaide: Tara specializes in structured finance and securitization transactions across various asset classes, representing a wide range of market participants.
specialties: M&A, Structured Finance, Secured Finance

name: Raleigh Johnston
bio: Raleigh provides practical and commercial advice to clients in connection with the tax issues of transactions involving REITs, real estate funds, and real estate partnerships. Raleigh Johnston focuses his practice on representing public and private companies, including REITs, in a variety of matters related to the tax and corporate structuring of investments in real estate. He advises clients on a wide range of U.S. federal income tax issues related to transactions involving REITs, corporations, partnerships, and limited liability companies. His practice includes regularly counseling clients in transactions involving joint ventures, fund formations, securities offerings, mergers and acquisitions, and real estate purchases and sales. He has a breadth of experience in advising clients on the tax and corporate aspects of investing in, acquiring, developing, selling, and leasing real estate assets.
specialties: M&A, Real Estate, P&S, Leasing, REITs, Federal, Partnership, Funds

name: Steven Penaro
bio: Clients can count on Steve to leverage his experience in state, federal, and appellate courts to guide them through many kinds of disputes resulting from and affecting commercial operations and business ownership. Steve Penaro focuses his practice on complex commercial litigation with an emphasis on cross-border disputes. Steve leverages his complex commercial litigation background and extensive trial experience to assist insurance clients seeking to underwrite contingent risk insurance products. (CV auf Deutsch) Steve has represented plaintiffs and defendants in a broad range of complex commercial disputes, including litigation involving breach of contract, fraud, right of first refusal claims, RICO, various tort claims, securities law, and agency and fiduciary relationships. Steve has represented clients in a wide array of industries, including the health care, retail, and insurance industries, where he has litigated a variety of issues unique to that space. His experience spans federal and state practices and appeals. Before joining the firm, Steve was a voluntary law clerk to the Hon. Richard J. Sullivan of the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York.
specialties: Securities, Commercial, Health Care, Appellate, Insurance, RICO

name: Kenneth Rothenberg
bio: Ken leads investment banks, broker-dealers, hedge funds, and other financial institutions through the acquisition and sale of distressed investments. Clients value his experience representing lenders on secured and unsecured financing transactions across a wide array of industries. Ken Rothenberg leads the Distressed Debt & Claims Trading Team, a niche and complex field requiring particular legal and financial expertise. With over 25 years' experience, Ken is one of the most trusted and recognized authorities on bank debt and claims trading matters. His advice is sought by the most sophisticated market participants in the par and distressed trading market for broadly syndicated loans, private credit, distressed securities, bankruptcy claims, claims arising from litigation proceedings, and other unique trading opportunities. Ken represents some of the largest and most well-known investment banks, market makers, credit funds, CLOs, and buyside entities in all aspects of loan and claims trading. Clients look to Ken in understanding primary and secondary market standards, conventions, and documentation for trading liquid and illiquid par and distressed bank loans and claims. Ken is also an active participant in the Loan Syndications and Trading Association (LSTA) and the Loan Market Association (LMA), sitting on numerous committees. He is a regular speaker for the LSTA on topics relating to primary and secondary loan market transactions and regularly assists the LSTA in updating its loan trading documentation utilized in the primary and secondary market.
jaide: Ken specializes in leading financial institutions through the acquisition and sale of distressed investments, particularly in the field of Distressed Debt & Claims Trading.
specialties: Structured Finance, Lending, Secured Finance, Debt Capital Markets, Broker-Dealer, Unsecured Finance

name: Timothy Trysla
bio: Tim's service in key policy positions at CMS guides critical business strategy for some of the nation's largest health care organizations. By keeping a watchful eye on the direction of the health care trade, he helps his clients navigate legislation, compliance and Medicare/Medicaid reimbursement matters. Timothy P. Trysla is a partner in the Health Care Practice Group, and serves as leader of the Health Policy Team. Tim has more than 30 years of health care policy experience in the private sector and executive and legislative branches of government. He served as chief of staff and former senior policy advisor at the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS). In addition to advising the administrator on matters of Medicare and Medicaid policy, he worked as a liaison for CMS to the White House and Congress on Medicare reform and prescription drug coverage issues. He served as administrator-designee and speechmaker to large medical specialty and beneficiary groups and helped manage the development, passage, and implementation the Medicare Part D program. Tim has experience counseling clients on numerous regulatory and transactional health care matters and investigations, Medicare and Medicaid reimbursement, and legislative strategy. He represents a diverse group of clients, including hospitals, investment banks, medical device manufacturers, PBMs, long-term care pharmacies, diagnostic suppliers, and durable medical equipment manufacturers. Tim has been invited to speak on pharmaceutical pricing, Medicare Advantage reimbursement, and health care reform by numerous provider and investment groups, such as Pharmaceutical Education Associates, Financial Research Associates' "Medicare Advantage Summit," and the Wharton Health Care Business Conference. He was named in The Hill's Top Lobbyist 2021.
specialties: Compliance/Regulatory, Medicaid, Medicare, Health Care, Policy, Legislative

name: Michael Scarpati
bio: Michael's practice spans all major areas of intellectual property law, including patent prosecution, due diligence investigations, district court litigation involving patents, trademarks, and copyrights, and post-grant proceedings before the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office. Michael's practice spans all major areas of intellectual property law, including patent prosecution, due diligence investigations, district court litigation involving patents, trademarks, and copyrights, and post-grant proceedings before the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (e.g., inter partes review, reexamination, and reissue proceedings). He has extensive experience drafting patent invalidity, non-infringement and freedom-to-operate opinions, conducting prior art searches, and analyzing patent portfolios. Michael has advanced degrees in the areas of molecular biology, which he leverages to draft and prosecute patent applications related to biotechnology, pharmaceuticals, diagnostics, agricultural products, medical devices, and related life sciences subject matter. Michael also has a degree in computer science, providing cross-disciplinary expertise necessary for the prosecution of software patents. In that regard, he has experience drafting and prosecuting patents directed to software and business methods that implement machine learning, blockchain-based ledgers and other cutting-edge technologies. In addition to the above, Michael also has several years of experience prosecuting and litigating utility and design patents related to consumer products technology, and has counseled clients regarding intellectual property issues at all stages of the product development life cycle. Michael has extensive experience working with a wide range of clients, including Fortune 100 companies, start-ups, and research universities, in the areas of biotechnology, software, consumer products, and other technological niches. His practice is primarily focused on helping clients develop, manage, and enforce patent portfolios and other intellectual property assets. To that end, Michael has also assisted clients with licensing and enforcement actions to defend and/or monetize their intellectual property assets, in state and federal court litigation. Prior to joining ArentFox Schiff, Michael was an attorney at a biotechnology-focused boutique based in New York. Before entering the legal field, Michael was an academic and research scientist and has extensive hands-on experience with biotechnology, with over a decade of experience working at the lab bench at university and private laboratories. His research interests include all aspects of molecular biology, with a particular focus on structural biology and signal transduction. He is familiar with both standard wet lab techniques and bioinformatics-based approaches. He is also familiar with machine learning and data science techniques, as applied to "big data" analyses for biological research and other fields of technology.
specialties: Litigation, Patent Prosecution, Pharmaceuticals, Trademark, Life Sciences, Patent Litigation, Copyright

name: Lucas Amodio
bio: Lucas Amodio is a partner in the firm's Intellectual Property practice group. The majority of his practice focuses on high-technology and computer-related matters involving patent law and cybersecurity. A former software engineer and patent examiner, Lucas guides in-house counsel, inventors and patent examiners in applying for and protecting U.S. and foreign patent applications. In a variety of fields, he counsels through the distinctive stages of product design and development to ensure consistency. Lucas is also a Certified Ethical Hacker (CEH) through the International Council of E-Commerce Consultants (EC Council). This certification enhances his ability to provide guidance about security flaws and techniques to protect data. Lucas actively participates in litigation support for disputes involving patents as well as in U.S. patent re-examination proceedings. He assists clients on patent infringement issues, preparation of non-infringement and invalidity opinions and patentability, due diligence, and patent landscape analyses. In these instances, Lucas conducts searches, evaluates the results and provides ideas on going forward or modifying designs to alleviate risk. As both a computer engineer and attorney, he deals with the issues that arise due to the increased interconnectivity and vulnerability of devices, i.e., the Internet of Things. Lucas frequently writes on the topics of cybersecurity, hacking and the Internet of Things. He publishes content on LinkedIn and has also been featured in Law360. To view recent articles, visit his LinkedIn page. Or follow him on Twitter @DarthAttorney. Prior to joining the firm, Lucas was a law clerk for the Office of Unfair Import Investigation.
specialties: Data Privacy, E-Commerce, Patent Prosecution, Technology Transactions

name: David Blank
bio: David is a partner in the Healthcare and Litigation & Dispute Resolution practices and a member of the Life Sciences industry team. He counsels individuals and entities through government investigations, advises on corporate compliance and governance matters, and litigates civil and white-collar healthcare fraud cases. David offers clients specialized expertise in healthcare enforcement and compliance counseling following a distinguished federal career with the Office of Inspector General (OIG). He provides clients with an unparalleled understanding of federal investigations and enforcement actions through his ability to replicate the government's case development strategies and interpretation of the governing laws, rules, and regulations. He also draws on his prior OIG experience to give clients insight into the government's expectations relating to corporate governance and compliance best practices. David routinely provides advice on fraud and abuse issues, including matters arising under the False Claims Act, the Anti-Kickback Statute, the physician self-referral law (Stark Law), federal healthcare reimbursement, and other regulations promulgated by CMS, DEA, FDA, or OIG. Clients often seek David's expertise to conduct internal investigations after receiving a government subpoena or civil investigative demand, or after self-identifying compliance concerns. At each step, he provides guidance on risk mitigation strategies, the implementation of compliance safeguards, and advice on navigating investigations, audits, overpayment returns, and OIG self-disclosures. David provides his depth of experience to a wide variety of healthcare clients including, physician practice groups, healthcare technology companies, pharmaceutical and device manufacturers, pharmacies, assisted living facilities, home health providers, laboratories, and individual healthcare providers and suppliers. Prior to working in private practice, David served as senior counsel for over a decade with OIG, where he represented the agency in healthcare fraud and abuse matters involving Civil Monetary Penalties Law (CMPL) and federal program exclusion law. He routinely resolved matters under the OIG's Self-Disclosure Protocol, negotiated and monitored Corporate Integrity Agreements, and litigated agency matters before departmental administrative law judges and appeal boards. During his government tenure, David served as OIG's Litigation Coordinator, Exclusion Coordinator, and subject matter expert for the Select Agent and Toxins Program. Through these positions, David helped shape OIG's investigative and enforcement efforts, laying the groundwork for the agency's subsequent formation of the Affirmative Litigation Branch, a division focusing solely on the imposition of monetary penalties and the exclusion of individuals and entities under the CMPL. During his federal career, David was designated a Special Assistant United States Attorney (SAUSA) in the Southern District of Mississippi. As a SAUSA, he served as the healthcare subject matter expert for the office and investigated and prosecuted matters from the grand jury process through sentencing. His work as a prosecutor resulted in numerous indictments and several jury trials, contributing to the conviction of 34 individuals. David was an instructor for the Academy for Civil Rights Investigators at DOJ's National Advocacy Center in Columbia, South Carolina, where he trained attorneys and investigators on case development and litigation techniques. He is a frequent speaker on topics related to healthcare fraud and abuse and is an active member of several healthcare industry associations.
specialties: Compliance/Regulatory, Stark, False Claims Act, Investigations, Government, Pharmaceuticals, White Collar, FDA

name: Gregory Gaylis
bio: Greg is a partner in the Healthcare practice and a member of the Hospitals & Health Systems, Post-Acute & Long-Term Care, and Healthcare Private Equity industry teams. Healthcare providers seek Greg's client-centric, practical approach to handling complex business transactions and regulatory compliance matters. Greg counsels healthcare providers and financial institutional clients (particularly private equity and REITS) on mergers and acquisitions, joint ventures, professional practice management models, corporate governance, and general contracting. His practice also focuses on healthcare regulatory and compliance matters including the corporate practice of medicine, the Stark Law, the federal Anti-Kickback Statute, Medicare and Medicaid conditions of participation and reimbursement, antitrust, and health information privacy. Greg is also a member of the firm's Change of Ownership ("CHOW") team that leads all regulatory aspects of healthcare transactions for investors, operators, managers, capital partners, and developers of all sizes and in all 50 states. The team streamlines the regulatory process so that clients close their transactions on or ahead of schedule. Whether obtaining licensure and Medicare/Medicaid approvals, structuring transactions to expedite closing, anticipating issues to minimize cash flow disruption, creatively resolving diligence issues, or advising on CHOW guidelines and compliance, the team provides extensive experience and practical solutions. To date, the CHOW team has served as primary regulatory counsel in transactions valued at more than 35 billion. Greg is active in his community and participates in various local organizations, including serving on the Executive Committee and board of the Jewish Educational Loan Fund, as co-treasurer at Congregation Beth Tefillah, and as co-chair of the Georgia Goal Committee at The Epstein School.
specialties: M&A, Compliance/Regulatory, Medicaid, Medicare, Stark, HIPAA

name: Michael Penney
bio: Mike Penney is co-head of Arnold & Porter's Capital Markets, Corporate Governance, and SEC Reporting practice. Mike advises on a full range of securities and corporate matters, including equity and debt offerings, M&A, joint ventures, venture capital and growth equity financings, and SEC reporting and compliance, primarily for clients in the technology, biotechnology, and financial services industries. His clients include: U.S.-based public issuers and foreign private issuers ranging from smaller reporting companies to well-seasoned issuers; investment banks and financial institutions; venture capital and private equity funds; and private companies in venture and growth equity financings. Prior to joining the firm, Mike served as General Counsel of Hercules Capital, a public BDC focused on investments in venture-backed and publicly traded life sciences and technology companies. Mike previously served as Vice President and Senior Counsel at State Street Corporation, where he led domestic and cross-border mergers and acquisitions, joint ventures and strategic investment transactions and capital raising transactions. Pharmacosmos on its 400 million tender offer and acquisition of G1 Therapeutics. Coherus on its all-stock acquisition of Sufrace Oncology. Enovix Corporation in its 100 million common stock primary offering, in its 250 million issuance of publicly-traded warrants and in its 350 million Rule 144A convertible notes offering, as well as on U.S. securities matters and mergers and acquisitions matters. Enphase Energy in multiple offerings of convertible senior notes, including its 1.13 billion convertible notes offering, and in its acquisitions of Clipper Creek, DIN Engineering, and Sofdesk. FTC Solar on U.S. securities matters, capital markets matters, and mergers and acquisitions matters. SunPower in its offerings of Rule 144A convertible bonds, its at-the-market offering facility, and its acquisitions of Sunder Energy and Ambia Energy.
jaide: Specializes in capital markets, securities and corporate transactions, including equity and debt offerings, M&A, joint ventures, and SEC reporting and compliance for technology, biotechnology and financial services clients.
specialties: Compliance/Regulatory, Capital Markets, Securities, Finance, M&A, Private Equity, Venture Capital, Joint Ventures, Energy, FTC

name: Kathleen Reilly
bio: Kathleen (Kate) Reilly is a partner in the firm's Securities Enforcement & Litigation practice. Kate represents financial services companies, audit and accounting firms, special committees, and public issuer and private companies in securities enforcement, commercial litigation, whistleblower, white collar, and government investigation matters. Kate has significant experience handling actions in state and federal courts, administrative proceedings, and arbitrations. She also routinely conducts internal investigations and defends clients in enforcement matters by the SEC, DOJ, PCAOB, State Attorneys General, and CFTC. A staunch advocate of pro bono work, Kate is a member of the firm's Pro Bono Committee and routinely takes on matters involving criminal defendants and educational rights. She also serves on the firm's Management Committee as well as the National Hiring Committee. Kate obtained her J.D. at the Villanova University School of Law, from which she graduated magna cum laude and was elected to the Order of the Coif. During law school, she served as an Associate Editor of the Villanova Law Review. Kate earned her B.A. in history and political science from Boston College, graduating cum laude.
jaide: Kate specializes in securities enforcement and litigation, representing financial services companies, audit and accounting firms, special committees, and public issuer and private companies in various legal matters.
specialties: Investigations, Criminal, White Collar, Banking, Arbitration, Securities, Commercial, Administrative, Whistleblower, Government

name: Lee Rosebush
bio: "Lee is strategic and practical in his advice." A defense, regulatory and registered patent attorney with experience as a registered pharmacist, Lee Rosebush counsels companies and executives as a strategic business advisor and serves as outside general counsel for pharmaceutical and pharmacy industry clients. His in-depth industry knowledge and experience working with biologics, medical device and healthcare companies gives clients a single source for regulatory, diligence and litigation counsel. Clients reach out to Lee as the first point of contact when confronted with legal issues related to development, operations, investment, marketing, advertising or product promotion. With post-graduate degrees in finance and business, Lee is frequently sought out to help expedite corporate deals involving healthcare entities. He also advises private equity and public and private companies in due diligence matters and buy-sell transactions. Lee's ability to smoothly shift between the legal, governmental and pharmaceutical environments further helps him to efficiently secure operating licenses or assist drug manufacturers avoid compliance actions from governmental agencies. Active with the Drug Quality and Security Act (DQSA), as well as the Federal Food and Drug Administration's (FDA) regulation of pharmacy compounding, Lee speaks and writes on both issues, and is passionate about orchestrating and advocating for pharmacists and pharmacies. He was recently named an Affiliate Professor at the University of Maryland School of Pharmacy. Additionally, Lee is the Leader of BakerHostetler's Pharmacy and Reimbursement team and Co-Leader of the FDA team.
specialties: Compliance/Regulatory, Licensing, Pharmaceuticals, Food/Beverage, Medical Devices

name: Eileen Boyce
bio: Eileen Boyce's practice focuses on public securities offerings, private placements, mergers and acquisitions and general corporate matters. She also advises clients on securities compliance issues, including Exchange Act reporting.
specialties: M&A, Securities

name: Daniel Leventhal
bio: Daniel Leventhal represents and counsels owners in connection with the acquisition and disposition of commercial properties, including multifamily, student housing and shopping center projects; developers and investors in connection with the development of office, multifamily, student housing and mixed-use projects; owners and investors in connection with the formation of ventures; owners and lenders in connection with debt financings and refinancings secured by industrial, retail, office, multifamily and student-housing projects, including land subject to ground leases; and landlords and tenants in connection with office, retail and industrial leases.
specialties: Finance, Leasing, P&S, Development, Landlord/Tenant, Construction

name: Steve Mann
bio: Steve Mann's practice focuses on the representation of both public and private companies in complex commercial and cross-border matters, with a particular focus on those relating to: Information Technology: software, licensing, distribution, integration and development transactions, whether on-premises, cloud-based, or virtualized Outsourcing: managed services relating to critical business functions such as IT, human resources, accounting, product manufacturing, and logistics Data Commercialization and Analytics: "big data" analytics platforms and the acquisition and sale of related datasets Emerging Technology: pharmaceutical research, joint development arrangements, including the treatment of inbound and resulting intellectual property Data Privacy and Security: compliance with, and conducting transactions subject to, privacy laws and regimes such GDPR, CCPA, PCI-DSS, and NYDFS regulations Complex Manufacturing: manufacturing and acquisition of assets such drilling rigs, commercial aircraft, custom components and related equipment Corporate Transactions: acquisitions, divestitures, investments, and similar matters relating to the above Prior to entering law practice, Steve worked for a leading technology company as a software systems architect and program manager overseeing the integration of acquired companies.
specialties: M&A, Cross-Border, Compliance/Regulatory, Pharmaceuticals, Public Company, Licensing, Technology Transactions, Investment Management, Cybersecurity

name: Michael Portillo
bio: Michael Portillo represents entrepreneurs and emerging companies in the technology, renewable energy, and life sciences industries at all stages, from formation and seed financing through development of the business and venture capital financings to the liquidity event. In his role as an outside general counsel, Michael serves as a trusted advisor to his clients, providing advice and support for governance matters, day-to-day operations and a variety of transactions, including financings, mergers and acquisitions, technology transfers and strategic partnerships. In addition to his company-side work, Michael also has significant experience representing venture capital and strategic investors in early-stage and growth-stage financings. Prior to joining Baker Botts, Michael was an associate in the Finance Group of another international law firm, representing lenders and borrowers in a wide range of financing transactions.
specialties: Energy, Finance, M&A, Venture Capital, Life Sciences, Technology Transactions, Emerging Growth

name: Margaret Welsh
bio: Margaret (Maggie) Welsh is a nationally recognized patent lawyer and Co-Chair of the firm's Artificial Intelligence team. As tech rapidly changes our daily lives, it also creates incredible legal challenges. For companies to thrive, they need a seasoned intellectual property attorney who can handle complex and evolving technology issues. To do so, companies increasingly turn to Maggie. Blending an engineering background and extensive experience with patent litigation, patent prosecution, and due diligence, Maggie quickly digs into the weeds while keeping an eye on human interaction and business needs to craft winning strategies. Maggie brings her passion for emerging technology to her role as a leader of the Artificial Intelligence Practice Team and host of Baker Botts' podcast, AI Counsel Code. In these roles, she helps companies navigate the complexities of using AI and developing AI technologies. Companies rely on Maggie to spot, analyze, and manage risk, in order to offer practical solutions to business priorities. She also helps clients avoid and navigate litigation by advising on the evolving tech legal landscape and on strategic advancements for a company's business. IAM 1000 (2024) praised Maggie for her "professionalism, hardwork and ability to identify potential risks for her clients."
specialties: Patent Litigation, Technology Transactions, Patent Prosecution

name: Jamie Yarbrough
bio: Jamie Yarbrough represents companies across a broad array of industries in mergers and acquisitions, securities offerings and general corporate concerns. Jamie has substantial experience assisting public companies in navigating complex transactions to achieve their strategic goals. He also advises clients on securities compliance issues, including Exchange Act reporting.
specialties: Securities, M&A, Public Company, Compliance/Regulatory

name: Matthew Chester
bio: In more than 20 years of practicing law in the private sector and at the Department of Justice, Matt has handled dozens of arguments, hearings, and trials in state and federal court, achieving successful results on behalf of his clients. In his decade at Baker Donelson, Matt has represented individuals and corporations in connection with a myriad of civil, criminal, and regulatory proceedings, as well as counseled clients on internal investigations. Among many other things, Matt has a wide-ranging criminal practice, representing individuals and corporate entities in matters involving criminal allegations of public corruption, fraud, health care violations, export control violations, environmental violations, immigration violations, and espionage. As an example of one of Matt's criminal matters, he represented a former public official in connection with malfeasance charges; after nearly four years of litigation, the case was recently dismissed for prosecutorial misconduct. Matt also has a diverse civil practice. He represents individuals and corporations in False Claims Act (FCA)/Qui Tam cases against both governmental trial attorneys and a myriad of commercial litigation disputes including tort matters, business disputes, breach of contract litigation, and products liability lawsuits. As an example of his civil work, Matt prosecuted a civil lawsuit, acting as a plaintiff, for fraud against an individual, ultimately obtaining a judgment of nearly 1 million. Additionally, as part of his civil practice, Matt acts as one of two primary Firm coordinating attorneys for a Fortune 20 company for its civil litigation needs across Baker Donelson's footprint. During his time at Baker Donelson, Matt has additionally assisted and counseled entities with a wide variety of other legal, investigatory, and litigation needs. For example, Matt has conducted internal investigations for publicly traded, Fortune 500 companies facing allegations of corporate wrongdoing. As another example, Matt was engaged to represent a federal district court's clerk's office in connection with a number of challenges associated with the jury selection processes utilized by that clerk's office. Matt's work resulted in a comprehensive brief and report that was adopted by numerous federal district court judges in connection with those jury selection challenges, who ultimately rejected such challenges – in rulings that were affirmed by the applicable federal appellate court. Prior to joining Baker Donelson, Matt worked at the Department of Justice as an Assistant U.S. Attorney (AUSA) in New Orleans, Louisiana. As an AUSA, Matt was a member of the Financial Fraud and Public Integrity units in the U.S. Attorney's office and was promoted to the position of Deputy Supervisor of the Public Integrity group. He spent most of his time as a federal prosecutor focusing on white collar crime, fraud, and public/official corruption prosecutions. Matt's experience includes multiple high-profile trials and convictions obtained against former elected and public officials. During those years as a federal prosecutor, Matt was honored as the recipient of multiple awards, including five Excellence in Law Enforcement Awards from the New Orleans Metropolitan Crime Commission for his work on public corruption prosecutions in and around New Orleans, as well as awards from the Internal Revenue Service, the U.S. Department of Homeland Security and the Social Security Administration Office of the Inspector General. Most notably, Matt was awarded the Department of Justice's Award for Superior Performance in Litigation in 2015 for his work in connection with a high-profile public corruption prosecution in New Orleans. Before serving as Assistant U.S. Attorney, Matt practiced at an international law firm based in Dallas, Texas, where he handled numerous matters involving federal securities, financial class actions, complex civil litigation, white collar criminal matters, and corporate compliance.
jaide: Matt specializes in representing individuals and corporations in a wide range of civil and criminal matters, with a focus on public corruption, fraud, health care violations, and internal investigations.
specialties: Commercial, Environmental, Product Liability, Appellate, Investigations, Government, Criminal, Health Care, False Claims Act

name: Derek Liu
bio: Derek Liu is a partner in Baker McKenzie's San Francisco office. Derek handles mergers and acquisitions, and other complex corporate transactions, and has signed transactions with an aggregate transaction value of more than USD 110 billion. Prior to joining Baker McKenzie, Derek practiced at two top-tier firms in San Francisco and New York. Derek has extensive experience in all forms of mergers and acquisitions, representing clients from strategic acquirers to private equity sponsors to venture-backed start-ups. He has handled a range of transactions from public company sale processes to asset carve-outs to SPAC acquisitions to purchases of distressed companies. He also has experience with minority investments, joint ventures and shareholder activism. His primary focus is in the technology and life sciences sectors.
specialties: M&A, Private Equity, Life Sciences, Joint Ventures

name: Brook Mestre
bio: J. Brook Mestre is a seasoned corporate and securities attorney who represents publicly and privately held multinational and domestic businesses in a wide range of corporate transactions. Brook has extensive experience advising purchasers and sellers in cross-border mergers and acquisitions transactions, counseling clients from the inception of a deal through any post-acquisition restructuring and integration matters that may arise. Brook also represents clients in capital markets transactions, including public offerings and private placements of both equity and debt, as well as on day-to-day governance and other corporate matters. His clients operate in a variety of industries, including technology, sports and entertainment, consumer products, healthcare, life sciences, social media, recreational vehicles, interstate transportation and logistics, energy exploration, production, and transmission, food services, and national defense.
specialties: Capital Markets, M&A, Cross-Border, Securities, IPOs

name: Qingqing Miao
bio: Qingqing Miao is a corporate attorney focusing on aviation and cross-border transactions. Qingqing acts as fleet counsel for U.S. airlines and advises global airlines, aircraft lessors, private aircraft owners, and banks on aviation equipment financing and trading, import and export, Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) contract matters, and deal restructurings. Qingqing is a skilled negotiator who leads major contract negotiations and deal closings. She also advises business clients in various industries such as life science, real estate, and manufacturing on business matters, including company formation, legal risk management, contract drafting, dispute resolution, and mergers and acquisitions. Qingqing also acts as outside general counsel to support the U.S. operations of multinational companies. Born and raised in China, Qingqing is uniquely well positioned to advise on complex matters involving the U.S. and China.
jaide: Specializes in aviation-focused corporate law, advising airlines, lessors, aircraft owners and banks on equipment financing, leasing and trading, OEM contracts, cross-border transactions and restructurings, and serving as fleet and outside general counsel.
specialties: Finance, Aviation

name: Gregory Pyle
bio: Greg Pyle advises clients in corporate governance, business, and transactional matters, with a focus on complex mergers and acquisitions, joint ventures, health care transactions, and providing outside general counsel services to successful private companies. Greg works across industries with particular experience in nonprofits, food and distribution, agribusiness, and heavy industry. Greg enjoys serving clients as outside general counsel and getting to know and understand successful businesses and nonprofits in the Northwest. He prides himself on providing practical advice and strategic insight and routinely handles corporate governance and strategic transactions, including debt and equity offerings, acquisitions, and multigenerational transfers. He also has unique skills in health care and life science matters. Greg represents clients across the health care delivery system from major research institutions and health information technology companies, to working with national senior living operators and investors. He spent time as a seconded attorney to Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center, where he negotiated its clinical trial and other sponsored research agreements with a variety of major life sciences companies.
specialties: M&A, Capital Markets, Life Sciences, Transactional, Food/Beverage, Joint Ventures, Non-Profit, Agribusiness

name: Michael Robotti
bio: Mike Robotti specializes in defending corporations and individuals in investigations and litigation involving the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) and U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Mike also focuses his practice on internal corporate investigations and complex civil litigation. Mike has broad experience in cases alleging securities fraud, health care fraud, sanctions violations, insider trading, theft of trade secrets, money laundering, public corruption, and other crimes. As a former federal prosecutor, Mike has extensive federal trial court and appellate court experience. He has tried multiple federal cases to verdict, and he has argued numerous times before the federal district court and court of appeals. Mike also serves as Co-Leader of the firm's Anti-Money Laundering (AML) Team. He advises financial institutions and businesses on their AML obligations under the Bank Secrecy Act (BSA), as well as other compliance requirements, with a particular focus on issues involving DOJ corporate enforcement policy, cryptocurrency, cannabis, and anti-corruption. Mike has authored several articles and spoken on several panels regarding these topics. Mike also is a contributor to Money Laundering Watch, Ballard Spahr's blog devoted to criminal and regulatory money laundering issues. As a member of the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Eastern District of New York for nearly eight years, Mike served as deputy chief of the International Narcotics & Money Laundering section and as senior trial counsel for the Business & Securities Fraud section. Mike was a member of the trial team that prosecuted Joaquín "El Chapo" Guzmán Loera—the leader of the Sinaloa Cartel, the world's largest drug cartel. For his work on that case, in 2019, he received the Attorney General's David Margolis Award for Exceptional Service, the DOJ's highest honor. He also led the investigation of Genaro García Luna, a former Mexican cabinet official, who was convicted of protecting the Sinaloa Cartel in exchange for bribes. In addition, while a federal prosecutor, Mike investigated and prosecuted complex and high-profile cases in a wide variety of subject matters. His cases included, among others, matters related to foreign public corruption and bribery, money laundering, corporate misconduct, securities fraud, BSA and Office of Foreign Asset Control (OFAC) violations, mail and wire fraud, tax fraud, pharmaceutical regulatory compliance, and international drug trafficking.
jaide: Specializes in defending corporations and individuals in DOJ and SEC investigations and related litigation, conducting internal corporate investigations, and advising on AML/BSA compliance and complex civil matters including cryptocurrency and anti-corruption issues.
specialties: Commercial, Securities, Investigations, Trade Secrets, Criminal, Appellate, Banking, Health Care

name: Alan Kelly II
bio: Al leverages his extensive experience as a real estate attorney both in private practice and as in-house counsel to efficiently provide prompt and practical counsel to his clients. His background as in-house counsel gives Al a unique appreciation for, and perspective on, the challenges his clients face in a dynamic and evolving marketplace. Al concentrates his practice on real estate. He advises clients on many facets of real estate law including acquisitions, leasing, development, and financing. Before joining Barack Ferrazzano, Al served as Vice President and Associate General Counsel of Care Capital Properties, Inc., a multi-billion dollar REIT focused on the post-acute sector created via a spinoff from Ventas, Inc. Al's primary duties included oversight of the structuring and negotiation of investments, as well as the resolution of portfolio management matters. Earlier in his career, Al was the Assistant General Counsel, Business Legal Affairs, of Ventas where he managed real estate transactions, both domestic and international, including acquisitions and joint venture agreements involving the development of medical office buildings and senior housing facilities.
specialties: Finance, P&S, Development, Leasing

name: Robert Morrow
bio: Bob is a constructive problem solver who learns and understands each client's business and goals. Bob's clients look to him to develop creative, business-minded solutions to complex legal problems. Bob is a partner in Barack Ferrazzano's Corporate & Securities Group. For more than a decade, Bob has concentrated his practice on mergers and acquisitions, strategic business planning, contract negotiation, and general corporate matters. He has counseled clients in a wide range of industries, including insurance, hospitality, luxury and retail, manufacturing, supply chain, real estate, and technology, creating innovative solutions to help grow and strengthen their businesses. Clients seek Bob's counsel on a variety of business matters throughout their life cycle, with an emphasis on domestic and international (including Asia and Europe) acquisitions and dispositions, joint ventures, other business combinations, and equity and debt investments. He also advises clients on a variety of legal and business issues, including day-to-day operations, strategic planning, corporate governance, employment issues, general business planning matters, and dispute avoidance and resolution. In addition, he provides guidance on appropriate corporate structures, entity formation, and drafting operating and shareholder agreements. Bob is a member of the BF Foundation. He also represents pro bono clients in a variety of corporate matters. Prior to joining the firm, Bob was an associate at Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom, where he focused on mergers and acquisitions, corporate governance, and other corporate and securities matters.
specialties: Private Equity, Securities, M&A

name: Robert Boller
bio: Rob Boller is a skilled commercial litigator who guides clients through complex disputes, principally in connection with investment activity and the capital markets. Rob has represented investment managers, institutional investors, principals, and businesses before courts and arbitration tribunals across the country. Rob also provides guidance on fiduciary duty obligations and compliance with securities laws.
jaide: Rob specializes in guiding clients through complex disputes related to investment activity and the capital markets, representing various entities before courts and arbitration tribunals nationwide.
specialties: Securities, Commercial, Arbitration

name: Daniel Cohen
bio: Daniel A. Cohen's practice encompasses decades of experience working with educational institutions and their athletics departments. A dynamic and well-respected lawyer, Dan guides educational institutions through complex Title IX and NIL matters, navigating universities through the intricate landscape of athletics compliance to help them remain in front of the rapidly evolving legal environment. Dan counsels universities, colleges and other educational institutions on Title IX athletics and sexual misconduct matters, athletics strategic planning, name, image, and likeness (NIL) compliance, litigation, sports law, labor and employment, NCAA compliance, school and university law, and the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA). Viewing legal risks from all angles, Dan has built comprehensive, legally-compliant House implementation structures for 10 schools in FBS conferences over the last year. With more than 30 years in college athletics, counting his time working in four athletics departments, Dan's Title IX practice is extensive. He performs athletics audits and compliance reviews, develops strategic planning initiatives, and represents schools in investigations, litigation, and appeals. Notably, Dan maintains an active NIL practice, including drafting NIL contracts and policies for college athletics departments, advising universities on legal and equitable aspects of House and NIL programs, and representing Division I institutions in NIL litigation. In Title IX sexual misconduct matters, Dan drafts policies and procedures, leads extensive sexual misconduct investigation and adjudication trainings, and conducts investigations. He also litigates Title IX athletics and sexual misconduct matters on behalf of schools, including defending colleges against class actions and investigations by the federal Office for Civil Rights. Dan has been appointed as a Special Assistant Attorney General by two states to represent their public universities in Title IX matters. Since 2019, he has represented universities in every Football Bowl Subdivision (FBS) conference, as well as college athletics departments across 37 states and Washington, D.C., including major research institutions in 28 of those states. Dan is a frequent industry speaker, giving presentations to the National Association of Collegiate Directors of Athletics (NACDA), the National Association of College and University Attorneys (NACUA), LEAD1, the National Association for Athletics Compliance, the College Athletic Business Management Association, Women Leaders in Sports, and various collegiate athletic conferences. He has also led Title IX sexual misconduct trainings for multiple state college systems and individual schools.
jaide: Dan specializes in guiding educational institutions through complex Title IX and NIL matters in the athletics compliance landscape.
specialties: Litigation, Compliance/Regulatory, Investigations, Education Law, Contracts, Sports

name: Jacob German
bio: Jacob German navigates public and private sector clients through the intricate landscape of government relations and municipal law. He orchestrates solutions across municipal finance, procurement law, and lobbying initiatives, ensuring client voices resonate at every level of government. Jacob's approach to advocacy combines legal acumen with strategic insight, transforming complex challenges into opportunities for growth and success. Clients benefit from Jacob's multifaceted experience in state and local government matters. He crafts tailored strategies for counties, cities, and towns, addressing issues that range from economic development to infrastructure to municipal bond structuring. His keen understanding of government processes allows him to guide clients through regulatory mazes such as public records requests to negotiations with state agencies – all with a focus on ensuring compliance and maximizing opportunities. In the legislative and executive branch arenas, Jacob's advocacy skills are impressive. He develops and implements strategies that shape policy and drive business improvement for clients across highly regulated industries. His work extends to the federal level, where he champions infrastructure funding, healthcare initiatives, and federal grant opportunities. At the state level, he focuses his work on gaming, alcohol, healthcare, technology, corrections, tax, licensing, public safety, and infrastructure. This comprehensive approach to legal counsel ensures that client interests are represented across all government branches.
jaide: Jacob specializes in government relations, municipal law, and lobbying initiatives, with a focus on navigating clients through regulatory processes and maximizing opportunities at all levels of government.
specialties: Compliance/Regulatory, Health Care, Legislative, Public Finance, Infrastructure, PPP, Lobbying, Municipal Law, Policy, Economic Development

name: Joel Meyer
bio: Commercial litigator and trial advocate Joel Meyer delivers business-minded dispute resolution strategies that support his clients' financial objectives both in and out of court. Joel is an experienced negotiator who understands claims' underlying commercial considerations and the impact a dispute may have on his client's bottom line and market position. With 20 years of experience, Joel has resolved complex disputes for clients that include entrepreneurs, fast-growth and middle market companies, multinational and international companies, and mature corporations. He helps clients bring and defend disputes before state and federal courts and administrative agencies across the country. A skilled trial attorney, Joel has taken on difficult disputes and prevailed before judges, juries and arbitrators alike. His experience includes litigating and trying complex civil disputes involving breach of contract, business torts, trade secrets, product liability, toxic torts, consumer class actions, defamation, construction and healthcare law. Notably, Joel is committed to resolving litigation in the context of each client's specific financial goals and operational concerns. Valued for his good judgement, Joel knows, from practical experience, that protracted litigation, trial and appeals are not always the optimal route to conserving his clients' resources and reputation. As a result, when it is in his clients' interest to settle, Joel is dedicated to realizing the result as quickly as possible.
specialties: Class Action, Trade Secrets, Commercial, Product Liability, Toxic Torts, Administrative, Health Care, Arbitration, Construction

name: Meredith Collins
bio: Private equity sponsors and their portfolio companies call on Meredith Collins for her experience advising clients through various types of transformative transactions, including structuring and negotiating acquisitions (both growth investments and change of control buyouts), mergers, and divestitures, with a focus on delivering practical solutions that advance business objectives. Her representation covers a broad range of highly regulated sectors, including digital/technology, financial advisory and healthcare services. She brings a deep understanding of the unique regulatory and operational considerations affecting healthcare and technology-enabled businesses, enabling her to anticipate challenges and drive efficient deal execution. In the healthcare services space, Meredith has particularly deep experience advising behavioral health companies, home health, hospice and private duty nursing, urgent care clinics, and a variety of physician practice management companies. Meredith also works with clients in healthcare adjacent spaces, who sell into providers and payors, such as healthcare consultants, billing/coding auditors, and revenue cycle management companies. Having both a corporate and healthcare regulatory background, Meredith provides unique value to her clients when negotiating healthcare transactions. In the non-healthcare services space, Meredith's representative experience extends to tech-enabled platforms, digital service providers, and other business-to-business solutions, as well as financial advisory services transactions in the accounting and actuarial space. Meredith primarily focuses on private equity middle market M&A transactions in amounts between 25 million and 500 million or greater. Clients value Meredith for her commerciality, attentiveness, clear mindset, communication and ability to navigate fast-paced, high-stakes negotiations while maintaining a focus on long-term value creation and partnership.
jaide: Meredith specializes in advising private equity sponsors and their portfolio companies through transformative transactions in highly regulated sectors, with a focus on healthcare services and technology-enabled businesses.
specialties: M&A, Compliance/Regulatory, Private Equity

name: Michael Gibson
bio: Michael Gibson represents companies, developers and investors in commercial real estate acquisitions and dispositions, leasing and financing matters. He also has significant experience representing borrowers and lenders in both traditional and non-traditional lending transactions, including unique knowledge in advising on commercial real estate transactions involving foreign investment strategies through the EB-5 Immigrant Investor Program. Michael works with clients from a variety of industries, with a particular focus within the hospitality industry. Representing clients in commercial lending and real estate transactions nationally and abroad, Michael's practice includes: Real Estate – Counseling developers and investors in large-scale commercial real estate transactions involving the purchase, sale and leasing of real property; representing companies in the financing and management of real estate used in their business operations; advising landlords and tenants in commercial leasing transactions involving office, retail, industrial, multi-family and large shopping centers. Sale-Leaseback – Counseling real estate funds, investors and businesses in sale-leaseback and net lease transactions in the U.S. and abroad.
