Working on market leading transactions, Scott's experience includes the financing of the largest single asset deal in New York City at the time, representing lenders on the development of experiential assets throughout the country, the acquisition of various ski resorts for Alterra Mountain Company, the acquisition of the Denver Broncos, and the sale of the Barclays Center (the latter two transactions also being part of the highest valuation for an NFL and NBA team, respectively, at the time). In addition to his transactional work, Scott regularly writes legal articles, teaches CLEs and provides commentary on current real estate issues. Scott has been recognized nationally as an "MVP - Real Estate" and a "Rising Star - Real Estate" by Law360, as well as a "Trailblazer" in the real estate industry by The National Law Journal.
jaide: Scott specializes in sophisticated real estate transactions, including finance, acquisitions, dispositions, and related matters across various sectors such as hospitality, sports, entertainment, industrial, office, senior housing, and energy.
specialties: Finance, P&S, Development, Hotels & Resorts, Hospitality

name: Thomas Balmat
bio: Thomas represents commercial real estate clients with acquisitions, sales, development, leasing, financing, and corporate structuring. Thomas brings a diverse commercial real estate background serving clients in the hospitality, resorts, retail, senior living, multifamily, industrial and investment banking industries. His practice includes assisting clients of all sizes, from small business owners and commercial tenants to real estate developers and corporate entities. While in law school, Thomas was a student attorney within the University of Colorado's Entrepreneurial Law Clinic. In this capacity, Thomas helped start-up companies and entrepreneurs with entity formation, financing, real estate and intellectual property issues. Now, as a practicing attorney, Thomas assists students within the Entrepreneurial Law Clinic as a supervising attorney. Prior to joining Holland & Hart, Thomas worked at a real estate and land use law firm in Denver.
specialties: Development, Finance, P&S, Leasing, Landlord/Tenant, Hospitality

name: Andrew Cummings
bio: Andrew Cummings is a litigation attorney in Holland & Knight's Newport Beach and Los Angeles offices where he focuses on various types of civil litigation, real estate and bankruptcy. Mr. Cummings has experience in state and federal courts handling matters related to business litigation, real estate disputes, bankruptcy and fraud cases. For the last two years, Mr. Cummings has served as co-chair of the firm's Public and Charitable Service Committee in the Newport Beach office, which promotes dozens of pro bono and community service representations and projects in Southern California. Prior to joining Holland & Knight, Mr. Cummings worked for a boutique litigation firm in Irvine, California. Previously, he worked for the Orange County office of a global law firm, where he was part of the business litigation team. Prior to law school, Mr. Cummings served as a sports director at the YMCA, where he developed and oversaw a variety of youth sports and community outreach events.
jaide: Andrew specializes in civil litigation, real estate, and bankruptcy law at Holland & Knight's Newport Beach office.
specialties: Commercial, Real Estate, Bankruptcy

name: Jennifer Duplissey
bio: Jennifer M. Duplissey is a lawyer in Holland & Knight's Dallas office and a member of the firm's Real Estate Practice group. Her practice focuses on commercial and agricultural real estate with a particular emphasis on commercial and agricultural leasing and real estate acquisitions and dispositions. Ms. Duplissey has 20 years of experience representing a wide range of clients with property matters nationwide, including investment, development and finance. She represents regional and national landlords, tenants, investors, trustees, fiduciaries, special servicers, pension funds, private equity firms, corporate clients, small businesses, individuals and others in connection with acquisitions, development, leasing, management, lending, dispositions and other matters related to farms, ranches, malls, shopping centers, office buildings, industrial buildings and build-to-suit properties. Prior to joining Holland & Knight, Ms. Duplissey was an attorney in the Dallas office of a prominent global law firm practicing in real estate and finance law. Ms. Duplissey has also served as general counsel for a regional retail and mixed-use development company, where she gained valuable experience in the intricacies of development and investment.
specialties: Development, Finance, P&S, Leasing, Construction

name: Yoni Fisch
bio: Jonathan "Yoni" Fisch is a New York real estate attorney who focuses on commercial real estate matters, including negotiating and structuring financing transactions and property acquisitions and sales. In addition, Mr. Fisch is experienced in advising on ground leasing, space leasing and subleasing across multiple sectors, including hospitality, retail, commercial and in connection with multiuse development. Mr. Fisch's representative matters include: representing the owner of a multiphase ground-lease development project that, when completed, will yield 100,000 square feet of newly constructed retail space for the client and approximately 1,000 affordable housing units for a development partner advising a borrower on the refinancing of a 250 million loan secured by a portfolio of multifamily high-rises in New York advising a borrower on the refinancing of a 15.5 million loan on a hotel property in Los Angeles representing the purchaser in the 29.5 million acquisition of a shopping center in the Chicago metropolitan area representing the landlord in the negotiation of two separate leases totaling nearly 60,000 square feet for car sales and showrooms in the New York metropolitan area representing the landlord in the negotiation of a hotel restaurant lease in a boutique hotel in New York
specialties: Finance, P&S, Development, Leasing, Affordable Housing, Landlord/Tenant, Hospitality

name: Morley Fortier III
bio: Morley S. Fortier III is a corporate attorney in Holland & Knight's Chicago office. Mr. Fortier focuses his practice on domestic and cross-border mergers and acquisitions (M&A), private equity transactions, equity financing matters, corporate governance issues and general corporate law matters for private equity funds and independent sponsors, their portfolio companies and other privately held businesses. In addition, Mr. Fortier has extensive experience advising clients through all stages of a company's life cycle, including initial capital structure, fundraising, acquisitions, divestitures, joint ventures and exit transactions. His experience covers a wide range of industries and deal sizes, and he routinely advises management teams and individual C-suite executives in equity-related matters. Prior to joining Holland & Knight, Mr. Fortier was an attorney at a global law firm in its Chicago office. While in law school, Mr. Fortier was lead articles editor for The John Marshall Law Review and a member of The John Marshall Law School Moot Court.
specialties: Finance, Cross-Border, M&A, Joint Ventures, Private Equity

name: Michael Jo
bio: Michael Jo is a transactional attorney and investment professional with an established track record of successfully originating and managing equity and debt investments. He has more than 19 years of experience structuring, negotiating, and closing complex commercial real estate transactions, while developing and strengthening client relationships. Mr. Jo represents investment companies, banks, and REITs and is a trusted advisor to C-level executives on legal and business risks. His real estate investment experience includes mortgage and mezzanine financing, as well as preferred equity and equity investing. He also has significant experience counseling lenders, special servicers, and investors with respect to workouts and investment management. Mr. Jo has a deep understanding of the legal and business issues that arise at both the commercial real estate loan and securitization transaction levels, including the legal aspects of post-securitization matters that include interactions with servicers and trustees.
specialties: Finance, Investment Management, REITs

name: Douglas Praw
bio: Douglas A. Praw is a partner in Holland & Knight's Los Angeles and Century City offices and serves as the West Coast Real Estate Practice Group Leader. His practice focuses on commercial real estate, including the representation of public and private REITs, public companies, commercial banks, hedge funds, private equity groups, educational institutions, and family office investors in connection with the acquisition, sale, development, leasing, ground leasing, and financing of a variety of asset classes, including office buildings, mixed-use projects, hotels, sports and entertainment districts, medical office buildings, multifamily and residential developments, industrial parks and retail centers. He also provides strategic advice on various public financing and public infrastructure projects. Mr. Praw holds his Series 63 and 82 licenses. For his extensive experience in a wide range of real estate and public finance matters, Mr. Praw has been selected for inclusion in Chambers USA: America's Leading Lawyers for Business and has been recognized as one of the top "40 Under 40" most successful and youngest stars of the Southern California real estate industry. Mr. Praw has also written articles published in American City & County magazine, Water Impact, The California Real Estate Journal, Western Real Estate Business and Builder and Developer.
specialties: Real Estate, Finance, Leasing, REITs, P&S, Development, Hotels & Resorts, Public Finance, Infrastructure

name: Colby Smith
bio: Colby W. Smith is a corporate attorney in Holland & Knight's Philadelphia office. Mr. Smith focuses his practice on a variety of business law matters, with an emphasis on middle-market merger and acquisition (M&A) transactions. Mr. Smith represents clients in a variety of industries, including technology, life sciences, healthcare, manufacturing, distribution and logistics, aerospace and defense, sports and entertainment, retail and consumer products. He represents seed, institutional and venture capital investors, with a focus on corporate venture capital investing. In addition, he represents private equity funds and their portfolio companies in general business law and transactional matters. Prior to joining Holland & Knight, Mr. Smith was an attorney at a large international law firm's Philadelphia office.
specialties: M&A, Private Equity, Venture Capital, Life Sciences

name: Michael Traube
bio: Michael Traube is a deal lawyer with substantive experience on a broad range of corporate matters, including public and private mergers and acquisitions, joint ventures, strategic investments, alliances and collaborations, SPACs, PIPEs, spin-offs, real estate transactions and leases, commercial transactions and services arrangements (including tech-focused arrangements), venture capital transactions, Section 363 sales, corporate restructurings, reclassifications and corporate finance. Michael has led cutting edge technology and media-focused deals around the world, including Europe, Asia and Latin America, for both local and international clients. "Michael demonstrates an exceptional technical knowledge of the law combined with a creative, practical and business-minded perspective." The Legal 500 Areas of Concentration Mergers & Acquisitions Joint Ventures Proxy Contests and takeover defenses Corporate Governance Private Equity Media, Technology & Commercial Transactions
specialties: Capital Markets, Restructuring, Cross-Border, M&A, Private Equity, Technology Transactions, Joint Ventures, Latin America

name: Brian Thompson
bio: Organizations and individuals need an attorney who understands their needs and concerns and who can guide them through their real estate transactional or legal matters. With nearly 20 years of experience representing companies, individuals, and organizations, Mr. Thompson is that attorney. Brian concentrates his practice on title, contractual, and construction-related issues, with an emphasis on litigation matters in Maryland and Washington, D.C. Brian is a Director at Jackson & Campbell, P.C. and serves as co-chair of the Real Estate Practice Group. Brian is also a Director in the Firm's Trusts & Estates Practice Group and serves as Chair of the Firm's Marketing/Social Media Committee. Brian represents companies, individuals, real estate agents, and real estate settlement companies in numerous litigation matters in Maryland and Washington, D.C. He has served as lead counsel in real estate litigation cases before the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia, the District of Columbia Court of Appeals, the Superior Court of the District of Columbia, the United States Bankruptcy Court for the District of Maryland, the Maryland Court of Special Appeals, and various trial courts in the State of Maryland. Additionally, Brian maintains a transactional practice, including counseling companies and individuals in transactions involving the District of Columbia Tenant Opportunity to Purchase Act and development. Brian currently serves on the Board of the D.C. Land Title Association, a role that he's had since 2014. In addition to his role on the D.C. Land Title Association Board, Brian has also served as President, Immediate Past-President, Secretary, Treasurer, and President-Elect. Additionally, Brian serves on the Membership, Congressional Liaisons, State Legislative/Regulatory Action, and Title Action Network Committees of the American Land Title Association. Previously, he served as an elected member and co-chair of the Real Estate, Housing, and Land Use Community of The District of Columbia Bar.
specialties: Construction, Litigation, Development, Landlord/Tenant

name: Allison Dearington
bio: Allison P. Dearington is a principal in the Hartford, Connecticut, office of Jackson Lewis P.C. Her practice is focused on employment litigation, including class and collective action defense, and preventive counseling. Allison represents employers in state and federal courts, before the Connecticut Commission on Human Rights and Opportunities, the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, and in arbitration. She defends a variety of employment claims including discrimination, harassment, retaliation, failure to accommodate, violations of First Amendment rights, wage and hour violations including misclassification claims and tip-credit claims, breach of contract, and denial of public accommodations. Allison also has an active advice and counsel practice, advising employers on a wide range of employment-related issues. In addition, she has experience drafting employee handbooks and workplace policies, conducting investigations into internal employee complaints, drafting contracts, and conducting training seminars. Prior to joining Jackson Lewis, Allison served as law clerk to the Judges of the Mashantucket Pequot Tribal Court. She also was an associate at a boutique labor and employment law firm in Hartford, CT that focused on the representation of municipal employers. During her undergraduate studies, Allison interned for the United States Senate's Health, Education, Labor and Pensions Committee. Allison previously served as chair of the Greater Hartford Junior League's Career Connections for Young Women Committee (CCYW). CCYW was focused on inspiring young women in the Hartford area to focus on career planning, financial independence and improving their academic performance.
specialties: Litigation, Counseling, Arbitration, Wage & Hour, Administrative, Class Action, EEOC

name: Jarrod Cone
bio: Jarrod Cone is an attorney in the Real Estate practice of Jackson Walker's Fort Worth office. Jarrod has experience representing investment funds, developers and corporate clients in all aspects of commercial real estate transactions, including single property and complex portfolio acquisitions and disposition and the financing and leasing commercial projects nationwide. Jarrod has also advised clients in connection with the real estate aspects of corporate mergers and acquisitions.
specialties: Finance, P&S, Leasing

name: Mario Dolan
bio: Mario Perez Dolan is an experienced corporate attorney who has represented clients in a broad array of transactional matters, including mergers and acquisitions, joint ventures, private equity investments, and corporate governance. In the mergers and acquisitions portion of his practice, Mario has represented both purchasers and sellers in negotiated transactions in a variety of industries, including, energy services and infrastructure, manufacturing, industrial, and energy transition. Mario has extensive experience representing public and private companies, private equity funds, and family offices, which provides him the background and understanding required to provide solutions to the unique challenges presented in each transaction. Mario's joint venture practice is largely focused on capital intensive energy, infrastructure, and real estate development projects. He has represented multiple investors and management teams in the formation and equity investment in projects or portfolio companies pursuing renewable energy, E&P, oilfield logistics, and infrastructure strategies. His real estate experience includes the negotiation and structuring of joint ventures and investment vehicles for the development and acquisition of projects in the office, multifamily, and hospitality sectors. In each representation, Mario takes pride in understanding his client's strategic goals and working towards achieving those outcomes in an efficient manner that focuses on client priorities. This approach has provided Mario the opportunity to develop long-term relationships with his clients and become a trusted member of the deal team.
specialties: Finance, Securities, Energy, M&A, Joint Ventures, Cross-Border, Private Equity, Venture Capital, Fund Formation

name: Michelle Smith
bio: Michelle Moore Smith handles complex real estate transactions on behalf of investors, private equity funds, financial institutions, and borrowers. From start to finish, she guides clients through acquisitions, dispositions, financing, leasing, and management of office buildings, shopping centers, condominium projects, and unimproved land. Michelle's deep and broad experience in office, industrial and retail leasing matters makes her ideally suited for counseling landlords and tenants in the preparation and negotiation of leases, amendments, subleases, and other leasing documents. She is particularly skilled at assisting property developers in their lease-up efforts at new office buildings and is proud to have been part of the teams responsible for creating 100% occupancy levels in two new signature skyline office buildings. Clients appreciate Michelle's sound business judgment and commitment to helping them achieve their business goals. When new buildings are part of mixed-use developments, Michelle finds sensible solutions, agreeable with the competing interests of each area of the development during lease negotiations. Real estate investors also turn to Michelle for guidance in the structure and formation of joint ventures and other investment vehicles for these mixed-use projects. Known for getting deals done, financial institutions and borrowers also engage Michelle to structure, document, and close construction, development, revolving, and term loans financing various types of commercial real estate.
jaide: Michelle specializes in handling complex real estate transactions for investors, private equity funds, financial institutions, and borrowers, including acquisitions, dispositions, financing, leasing, and management of various types of commercial real estate properties.
specialties: Finance, Construction, P&S, Leasing, Development, Landlord/Tenant, Condo Associations

name: Jenna Ross
bio: Jenna Ross helps clients ranging from Fortune 500 companies to privately held businesses and individuals resolve high-stakes disputes. She has substantial experience litigating claims involving breaches of contract, fraud, business torts, and anticompetitive or unfair practices in both court and arbitration. Clients in the technology and financial services space, in particular, turn to Jenna when they are navigating disputes involving highly sophisticated and complex products and transactions. From pre-litigation negotiation to trial and appeal, Jenna is a zealous advocate who always keeps client objectives at the forefront. She approaches advocacy with a deep understanding of the different strategic goals of litigation and how to achieve them. In some cases, she works to minimize business disruption and avoid reputational harm. In others, she uses litigation as a tool to shape a client's future business opportunities. In every case, Jenna prioritizes clear and consistent communication with clients. She is adept at framing the big picture succinctly and in the language of business, analyzing claims or expected counterclaims in a way that informs confident decision-making. Jenna gained inside knowledge of how courts decide cases as a judicial law clerk. She calls on that experience when developing and delivering legal arguments. She digs deep and quickly masters the details of the case, often spotting legal issues or facts she can use to her client's advantage. Jenna's active pro bono practice focuses on reproductive rights advocacy. She serves on Jenner's Post-Dobbs Task Force and regularly counsels clients on how to navigate the dynamic legal landscape resulting from the Dobbs decision.
specialties: Commercial, Arbitration, Antitrust, Appellate, Unfair Competition

name: Emily Cai
bio: Emily Cai represents private equity funds and public and private companies in complex domestic and cross-border M&A and private equity transactions. Emily focuses her practice on the representation of private equity funds and privately held companies in leveraged buyouts, divestitures, carve-out transactions, recapitalizations, restructurings, and joint ventures. She also regularly counsels private equity firms and their portfolio companies on corporate governance and general corporate matters and has experience advising clients on growth equity investments including preferred equity investments, secondary sales, debt and equity financings, co-investments, and other types of investments and strategic transactions. Emily has advised clients on domestic and cross-border transactions in the United States, Greater China, Canada, Europe, South Asia, Latin America, and Africa. She has represented clients in a wide range of industries, including energy, infrastructure, manufacturing, technology, telecommunications, and consumer goods. Some of the clients Emily has represented include AGIC Capital, Brookfield, The Carlyle Group, Cortec Group, EagleTree Capital (f.k.a. Wasserstein & Co.), J.F. Lehman & Company, Koch Equity, MassMutual, Morgan Stanley Capital Partners, NOVA Infrastructure, One Equity Partners, and Verizon. Emily is a 2025 ALM "Young Lawyer of the Year (Corporate)" finalist and has been recognized by The Legal 500 as a key lawyer in the private equity buyouts area in 2022, 2023, and 2024. Emily is fluent in Mandarin and Cantonese and is qualified to practice PRC law.
specialties: M&A, Private Equity, Joint Ventures, Public Company, Cross-Border, Asia

name: Peter Devlin
bio: For nearly two decades, Peter Devlin has advised clients in connection with transformative financing transactions and day-to-day disclosure and corporate governance matters. Peter's broad experience ranges from global, Fortune 500 manufacturing companies to emerging life sciences companies, providing him with deep knowledge and perspective across the full spectrum of public and private securities offerings and with respect to critically important disclosure and governance issues. In addition to U.S. companies, Peter has significant experience advising international clients on U.S. securities laws, corporate governance matters, Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) reporting requirements, and stock exchange rules and regulations, including guiding several foreign private issuers through the legal and practical aspects of the public offering process in the United States. Peter has worked with issuers across many different industries, including agricultural technology, biotechnology, financial technology, financial services, insurance, transportation, shipping, consumer products, manufacturing, telecommunications, and education. His transactional experience includes complex capital markets transactions, including initial public offerings (IPOs), follow-on equity offerings (primary and secondary), private placements and private investment in public equity (PIPEs), and a range of debt transactions (investment grade, high yield, and convertible). Peter also advises on U.S. securities aspects of merger and acquisition transactions, reverse mergers, and go-private transactions. Peter is an advisory board member of the School of Science at Manhattan University.
jaide: Peter specializes in advising clients on transformative financing transactions, disclosure, and corporate governance matters across a wide range of industries and international securities laws.
specialties: Finance, Compliance/Regulatory, M&A, Securities, Life Sciences, Capital Markets, IPOs, Telecommunications

name: Nina Yadava
bio: Nina Yadava has almost two decades of experience defending public companies and their officers and directors in shareholder litigation and co-leads the Firm's Securities Litigation & SEC Enforcement Practice. She also has extensive experience representing companies, including major financial institutions, in complex contractual disputes primarily related to structured products and business combinations. Chambers describes her as "very talented," "highly intelligent and great with clients and adversaries." Nina was named one of the top six securities litigators in the country under 40 years old by Law360 in 2018. She has represented a variety of Fortune 500 companies, along with their officers and directors, in federal securities class actions lawsuits and related shareholder litigation. In addition, she has represented major banks in federal and in state court litigation in New York related to financial products including collateralized debt obligations, credit default swaps, and residential mortgage-backed securities. Nina was resident in London from 2011 to 2012 and was part of the team that successfully defended Texas Keystone in a 1.6 billion oil and gas dispute in the English Commercial Court related to a New York law contract for which Jones Day garnered such accolades as Litigation Team of the Year at the British Legal Awards, The Lawyer Awards, and the Legal Business Awards. Nina maintains an active pro bono practice and secured protected status in the United States for a young Honduran woman after a multi-day trial. She is also a member of the board of directors of Summer Search NYC, a national youth development nonprofit.
jaide: Specializes in securities litigation and SEC enforcement, defending public companies and their officers and directors in shareholder suits and handling complex contractual and financial‑products disputes.
specialties: Commercial, Securities, Banking, Class Action

name: Goody Agahi
bio: Goodarz (Goody) Agahi is a Corporate partner. His practice focuses on complex business transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, leveraged buyouts, recapitalizations, and financings. Goody has structured, negotiated and completed transactions in a wide variety of industries, including, apparel, consumer products, medical devices, pharmaceuticals, food and beverage, software, and technology.
specialties: M&A, Restructuring, Finance

name: Michael Creta
bio: Michael Creta is a partner at the firm, concentrating his practice on complex commercial disputes, consumer class action defense, disputes arising from employment relationships, trade secret litigation, and international arbitration. Mike works closely with clients to develop dispute resolution strategies to address their unique legal risks and needs. He has litigated matters in both state and federal courts across the country, as well as in domestic and international arbitrations. Mike has significant experience with defending consumer class actions against global retailers, financial services institutions, and other large companies. These actions frequently involve claims alleging unfair business practices, false advertising, consumer fraud, breach of warranty, breach of contract, and violations of various state and federal statutes. Mike's class action defense experience includes representing a global online retailer in numerous putative class actions involving allegations of false and misleading product labeling. Mike also has extensive experience with matters involving alleged trade secret misappropriation and violations of restrictive covenants. Through these matters, Mike has helped clients successfully obtain injunctive relief to protect their sensitive business information. Mike's trade secret litigation experience includes the representation of a medical diagnostic products manufacturer in an international arbitration, which resulted in an award in the manufacturer's favor. Outside of litigation, Mike has advised clients on best practices for protecting their proprietary information.
jaide: Michael Creta specializes in complex commercial disputes, consumer class action defense, employment disputes, trade secret litigation, and international arbitration.
specialties: Trade Secrets, Class Action, Labor & Employment, Unfair Competition, Commercial, Arbitration

name: Barrett Price
bio: Barry Price is a corporate partner in the Charlotte office and his practice focuses on mergers and acquisitions, private equity and leveraged buyouts with respect to regional, national and international corporate transactions. He is often involved in cross-border transactions, involving parties and operations in multiple countries and related international issues. Barry also advises corporate clients regarding general corporate matters, corporate governance and commercial contracts. Barry has represented numerous types of parties in transactions, including multinational public companies, private equity sponsors, financial institutions, private companies, founders and management teams. Barry has experience with a wide-range of industries, including logistics, business services, data management, distribution, green energy, financial services, healthcare, life sciences (including pharmaceuticals), manufacturing, software, technology, telecommunications and industrial services.
jaide: Barry specializes in mergers and acquisitions, private equity, and corporate transactions across various industries, including cross-border transactions and general corporate matters.
specialties: Telecommunications, Private Equity, M&A, Cross-Border, Energy, Life Sciences, Public Company

name: Carly Hepburn
bio: Carly's practice focuses on real estate finance and development, particularly HUD-insured loan programs and affordable housing. In addition to closing HUD-insured multifamily and healthcare loans, Carly also has asset management experience, including loan servicing, handling construction draws, project performance, and financial analysis. Before joining Kantor Taylor, Carly served as an Associate at Reno & Cavanaugh, PLLC. Prior, Carly spent seven years at Love Funding Corporation, a leading HUD-insured lender, where she served as Assistant Vice President and Associate General Counsel, and before that as an Asset Manager. Carly received her J.D. from Syracuse University College of Law. She received a B.A. in Policy Studies from Syracuse University. Carly is admitted to the New York State and District of Columbia bars. She is a member of the American Bar Association. Carly lives in Skaneateles, New York with her husband and twins.
jaide: Carly specializes in real estate finance and development, particularly in HUD-insured loan programs and affordable housing.
specialties: Finance, P&S, Development, Construction, Affordable Housing

name: Ava Alim
bio: Ava Alim is a partner in the firm's Real Estate group, advising financial institutions on complex commercial real estate transactions nationwide. Her practice focuses on originating loans for the commercial mortgage-backed securities (CMBS) market and includes a broad range of financial instruments such as securitized financings, mortgages and mezzanine loans. Ava guides clients — including major banks, commercial lenders and institutional investors — through the origination, purchase, sale and disposition of senior and subordinate debt secured by office buildings, hotels, multifamily complexes and other commercial properties. Known for her efficiency and practical, solutions-oriented approach, Ava consistently delivers seamless results aligned with her clients' business goals and timelines. She also represents landlords in commercial leasing matters, offering strategic counsel on lease negotiations and portfolio management. Ava is recognized for her collaborative style and ability to foster strong relationships with all parties involved in a transaction. Clients value Ava's responsiveness, attention to detail and commitment to achieving their objectives. She is dedicated to ensuring every transaction is well-structured and efficiently executed. Ava has been recognized by Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch for Real Estate Law (2024–2026) and received the Outstanding Pro Bono Service award in October 2022.
jaide: Ava specializes in advising financial institutions on complex commercial real estate transactions nationwide, with a focus on originating loans for the CMBS market and guiding clients through debt transactions secured by various commercial properties.
specialties: Leasing, P&S, Finance, Landlord/Tenant, Hotels & Resorts

name: George Carter
bio: George Carter focuses his practice on commercial real estate finance with an emphasis on originating loans secured by commercial real estate. He has significant experience representing banks and commercial lenders in connection with the origination of loans for the commercial mortgage-backed securities (CMBS) market as well as traditional balance sheet loans. He is known for helping clients navigate complex legal questions and situations and doing so in an efficient and timely manner which minimizes unnecessary conflict and expense for his clients.
specialties: Finance, Capital Markets, P&S

name: Joshua Feiger
bio: Josh Feiger advises companies and individuals buying, selling and investing in businesses. Josh focuses on structuring, negotiating and managing mergers, acquisitions, dispositions, equity financings, and other complex transactions. Josh has vast experience representing private equity funds, family offices, independent sponsors, founder-owned businesses and public companies through mergers, acquisitions, dispositions, equity financings, other complex transactions, and regarding general corporate matters. Josh has represented clients across a variety of industries, including manufacturing, industrials, business services, health care, financial services, food and beverage, technology and real estate. His transactions have ranged in size from several million dollars to more than five billion dollars. Josh takes a business-minded approach to his practice, serving as a valuable advisor and focusing on specific client needs for each situation. Josh began his career in the Chicago office of an international law firm.
jaide: Josh specializes in advising companies and individuals on mergers, acquisitions, dispositions, equity financings, and other complex transactions across various industries.
specialties: M&A, Private Equity, Finance, Public Company, Food/Beverage

name: Rachel Riley
bio: About Rachel M. Riley Rachel Riley focuses her practice on federal criminal defense. She has successfully prevented indictments, gotten charges dismissed and obtained favorable sentences for clients. Notably, Rachel also has earned acquittals in federal court on multiple counts of fraud. Her practice focuses on white-collar criminal defense, regulatory enforcement matters and internal investigations. Rachel has represented clients in matters involving health care fraud, embezzlement, securities fraud, public corruption and various other economic crimes. She regularly appears before the Department of Justice, the SEC and other state and federal agencies. She also served as federal trial counsel and argued dispositive motions in both state and federal court. Knowing the prosecutor's business and the client's is vital to the defense Rachel has worked for numerous former federal prosecutors and understands how the Department of Justice approaches a case. While sworn-in as an Assistant District Attorney on a three-month special assignment to the Dallas County District Attorney's office, Rachel tried numerous cases to verdict, gaining valuable experience as a prosecutor. She recognizes that knowing her client's business is key to building a defense against charges. It also is important in order to properly advise a company or individual on how to avoid problems in the future. During law school, Rachel was a staff editor for The SMU Science and Technology Law Review. She also interned for the Honorable Judge Jane Boyle in the US District Court for the Northern District of Texas and for the Honorable Justice Elizabeth Lang-Miers in the Texas Fifth District Court of Appeals.  Expand to read more Practice Focus Litigation White collar and internal investigations Health care Representative Experience Second-chaired a federal health care fraud case alleging Anti-Kickback violations and violations of the Controlled Substance Act. Obtained acquittal for the client, a physician, on seven counts. Represented a marketer in a 400 million health care fraud scheme, obtaining a favorable sentence for the client. Represented insurance business owner during a multiple-week federal bribery trial. Drafted and successfully argued a motion to suppress in a federal case that was featured on the front page of the Dallas Morning News. Obtained the dismissal of two counts for the client and his immediate release from jail. * First chair of bench trial in breach of contract dispute. Secured win. * Represented client charged via complaint with theft at the state level. Obtained a no-bill from the grand jury. * First chaired multiple misdemeanor criminal trials at the Dallas County District Attorney's Office. * Represented key witness charged with conspiracy in a large-scale public corruption case. Obtained sentence of probation. * Represented large retailer in conducting an internal investigation regarding a matter under FINRA's jurisdiction. * Represented large financial services company in conducting an internal investigation regarding a matter under the SEC's jurisdiction. Also advised in defense of a shareholder class-action lawsuit alleging securities law violations. * Represented individual charged with criminal violations of the FDCA for the promotion and sale of synthetic marijuana. * Represented dietary supplement company owner under investigation for alleged criminal FDCA violations. * Represented executive in alleged health care fraud scheme. * Represented professional athlete in alleged criminal tax evasion scheme. * Represented federally insured financial institution in relation to a cybersecurity breach and the compromise of personally identifiable information. * Represented industrial gas company in internal investigation regarding a special inquiry issued by the US Department of Energy Inspector General in conjunction with the Defense Contract Audit Agency concerning the alleged misuse of government contract funds. * * Experience prior to Katten News March 26, 2021 Katten Corporate, Litigation and Tax Attorneys Named to Super Lawyers 2021 Texas Rising Stars List December 16, 2020 Texas Super Lawyers Honors Five Katten Attorneys as Top Women, Rising Stars December 1, 2020 Katten Team Successfully Obtains Acquittal for Doctor on Health Care Fraud Charges October 19, 2020 Katten Attorneys Selected for 2020 Texas Super Lawyers List August 20, 2020 Katten Attorneys Distinguished as Top Legal Talent in the 2021 Best Lawyers in America and Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch Lists March 6, 2020 Katten Attorneys Selected to 2020 Texas Rising Stars List by Super Lawyers February 5, 2020 Katten Attorneys Chosen for Top Women Texas Super Lawyers, Rising Stars Lists September 3, 2019 Katten Attorneys Chosen for 2019 Texas Super Lawyers List March 12, 2019 Katten Attorneys Named to 2019 Texas Rising Stars List by Super Lawyers View All News
specialties: Investigations, Health Care, Commercial, Securities, Criminal, White Collar

name: Adam Stoddard
bio: Adam Stoddard is a partner in Katten's Real Estate practice with a broad background in structuring and executing all types of commercial real estate transactions, while emphasizing efficiency and attention to detail. He routinely represents clients in acquisitions, construction, development, dispositions, leasing, financing and servicing across the commercial real estate sector. Adam has substantial experience advising his clients on a wide scope of asset classes, including subdivision development, multifamily properties, retail centers, hotels, resorts and industrial properties. His practice includes a range of commercial leasing, lending and financing transactions representing real estate owners and operating companies, developers, debt funds, asset managers and commercial banks in the real estate market throughout the United States. Adam advises clients of all sizes in negotiating and structuring commercial mortgage loan originations, syndicated and participated credit facilities, commercial leases and construction and development projects. While attending law school, Adam was a member of the Wake Forest Law Review and the American Bar Association National Appellate Advocacy Moot Court Team.
specialties: Leasing, P&S, Development, Construction, Finance

name: John Funderburk
bio: John Funderburk practices in the litigation and construction groups. He represents clients in energy, commercial, environmental, transportation, and construction litigation in both state and federal courts. Prior to joining the firm, John practiced with a global law firm from its Houston, Texas office. John has represented both individuals and corporations in complex environmental litigation, contract disputes, maritime actions, personal injury and wrongful death lawsuits, deceptive trade practices claims, and oilfield contamination litigation. In addition, he coordinates with clients and experts to respond quickly to accidents and incidents across the country, helping clients preserve and quickly assess relevant evidence. He has represented clients across a broad range of industries, including energy, shipping, technology, and manufacturing.
specialties: Construction, Health Care, Transportation, Labor & Employment, Environmental

name: Gonzalo Mon
bio: Gonzalo Mon advises a wide range of companies in all aspects of promoting their brands. Companies turn to him when they need help creating their advertising campaigns, negotiating marketing agreements, and resolving disputes with competitors and regulators. Gonzalo helps his clients determine what they can say about their products and services in ads, as well as what type of evidence they need to support their claims. He has defended clients when their ads have been challenged by competitors and regulators, including in proceedings before the National Advertising Division (NAD). He has also helped clients challenge their competitors when those competitors made claims they could not support. Gonzalo drafts and negotiates many of the agreements that underlie his clients' marketing campaigns. Among other things, he has worked on sponsorship agreements, advertising agency agreements, and endorsement agreements with actors, athletes, musicians, and other influencers. Balancing his knowledge of the law and his client's business goals, Gonzalo knows how to find a middle ground in negotiations and get to a favorable outcome. As companies experiment with new strategies to promote their brands, Gonzalo helps them identify and address the issues raised by these strategies. For example, he has advised clients on how to run various types of campaigns—including sweepstakes and contests—on social media, how to work with influencers, and how to engage in "green marketing." Gonzalo takes the time to understand his clients' business goals, and he is known for helping to find practical solutions tailored to each client's risk tolerance, especially in cases where the answers aren't clear. As one client recently reported to The Legal 500 US, Gonzalo does, "an excellent job of balancing the legal risk and the business exigencies." Gonzalo knows firsthand what in-house counsel and business executives require to do their jobs effectively. He spent more than three years working onsite in a technology client's legal department, where he reviewed the majority of the company's advertisements, drafted agreements, advised on product launches, and assisted with various promotions. This experience helped him better understand his clients' needs. As one client recently told Chambers USA, Gonzalo "approaches all issues with intelligence and patience and is always sensitive to the needs of our business" In addition to his frequent articles and speaking engagements, Gonzalo serves as editor of the firm's Ad Law Access blog. "I work with clients who are creative, and understand that they often need resourceful and inventive solutions to the challenges they face."
specialties: Advertising

name: Chase Medling
bio: W. Chase Medling focuses his practice on various aspects of complex commercial litigation, including business torts, copyright and trademark infringement, securities, constitutional law, DTPA, and breach of contract. Mr. Medling has assisted individuals, business entities, and municipalities in connection with bringing lawsuits, negotiating settlements, and preparing for mediations and trials. Mr. Medling has first-chair trial experience in civil and criminal matters. In addition, he engages in family law matters, including divorces and child custody disputes. Mr. Medling is involved with pro bono family law matters and has volunteered in Texas Lawyers for Texas Veterans' legal clinics.
specialties: Arbitration, Commercial, Intellectual Property, Trademark, Securities, Copyright, Criminal, Family Law, Government

name: Daniel Ferris
bio: Daniel J. Ferris feels privileged to be called upon to assist companies and individuals across a range of industries and with a wide variety of issues. Dan handles matters involving class action, commercial disputes, corporate law and investigations, employment law, insurance, licensing, litigation, products liability, and professional liability defense, and has particular experience in non-compete and sales representative contracts. Dan handles high-stakes and high-profile lawsuits in both trial and appellate courts, in addition to state administrative hearings. His industry-specific experience includes: Representing corporations and individuals involved in health care, including corporations that administer state and federal programs such as Medicare and Medicaid, dentists, hospitals, medical device manufacturers, and physicians, and serving as Co-Chair of the Professional Liability & Health Care Section of Michigan Defense Trial Counsel Representing automotive manufacturers and serving as a member of the Original Equipment Suppliers Association Young Leadership Council Dan has been recognized by Michigan Lawyers Weekly as a "Leader in the Law," by Best Lawyers in America (a distinction earned by no more than 5.3% of lawyers in the United States), and by Super Lawyers as a "Rising Star" (a distinction earned by no more than 2.5% of Michigan lawyers). He was elected into the Fellows of the American Bar Foundation (a distinction limited to 1% of Michigan attorneys) and the Michigan State Bar Foundation Fellows Program (a distinction limited to 5% of Michigan attorneys). Dan is passionate about education, is a lifelong learner, and works hard to stay at the leading-edge of knowledge, training, and tactics in the legal profession so as to provide clients with the best service, work product, and results possible. In addition to his undergraduate and law degrees from the University of Michigan, he completed a trial advocacy program at the Northwestern University School of Law. Dan has served as an instructor at the University of Michigan and as an editor of Michigan Law of Damages and Other Remedies, a treatise published by the Institute of Continuing Legal Education. He has served as a member of Kerr Russell's Continuing Legal Education Committee and as a judge for moot court competitions at the University of Michigan and Wayne State University Law Schools. Dan has also served as a "Barrister" in the American Inns of Court, which is an organization that promotes legal education. He has presented on topics relating to commercial litigation, health care, and legal research. In recognition of his vision, initiative, and leadership skills, Dan was appointed to serve on Kerr Russell's Strategic Planning Committee. Dan is also passionate about civic service. He was first appointed and then elected to serve as a member of the State Bar of Michigan Representative Assembly, which is the final policy-making body of the State Bar. As Chair of the Drafting Committee, Dan worked closely on a proposal that was adopted by the Michigan Supreme Court and constituted the most significant reform of the Michigan Court Rules in more than 30 years. Dan is a graduate of Leadership Detroit, a nine-month, transformational leadership program hosted by the Detroit Regional Chamber and designed to challenge emerging and existing leaders from Southeast Michigan to bring about positive change in their communities. He is also a Fellow of the Oakland County Bar Foundation, which promotes increased access to and understanding of the legal system. Dan has supported homeless youth as a member of the Covenant House Michigan Associate Board. He additionally has volunteered with Kerr Russell's Giving Back program, has represented nonprofit corporations, and has represented clients on a pro bono basis. Dan previously held a leadership position in a community service organization and has traveled to multiple sites around the United States to perform volunteer work. Dan resides in Franklin and serves on the Village Council.
jaide: Dan specializes in handling high-stakes and high-profile lawsuits in various industries, including health care, automotive, and professional liability defense.
specialties: Investigations, Administrative, Class Action, Insurance, Labor & Employment, Appellate, Commercial, Product Liability, Health Care, Professional Liability

name: Robert Griest
bio: Robert Griest is a partner in King & Spalding's Atlanta office and is a member of the firm's Business Litigation group. Robert's practice focuses on representing businesses in complex commercial litigation, including defending class actions and litigating high-stakes business disputes. Robert has extensive experience defending companies in significant consumer class actions, including numerous multidistrict litigation (MDL) matters, with an emphasis on data breach and other consumer privacy cases, Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) claims, and alleged violations of consumer protection statutes. Robert also represents clients in complex business cases, including breach of contract actions and post-deal disputes. In addition to litigation, Robert advises clients on insurance coverage matters relating to cyber, directors and officers (D&O), professional liability/errors and omissions (E&O), and other lines of insurance. Robert earned his law degree from the University of Georgia School of Law, where he graduated cum laude and served as a member of the Managing Board of the Georgia Law Review. Robert received his bachelor's degree from Princeton University.
jaide: Robert specializes in representing businesses in complex commercial litigation, including defending class actions and litigating high-stakes business disputes.
specialties: Class Action, Insurance, Commercial, Data Privacy

name: Andrew Stakelum
bio: Andrew Stakelum is an energy disputes partner in King & Spalding's Houston Office. His focus on the energy industry includes the oil and gas, renewables, and refining sectors. A key aspect of Andrew's energy practice involves helping clients navigate the highly regulated environments in which they operate. Offshore Energy. Andrew represents maritime and offshore energy clients in disputes that range from traditional blue water matters to disputes involving the decommissioning of offshore energy infrastructure. His matters frequently involve disputes among working interest owners, vessel owners and charterers, and governmental authorities. He advises clients and has litigated claims under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act, the Clean Water Act, the Oil Pollution Act, and their implementing regulations. Onshore Energy. Andrew represents a wide range of onshore energy clients in traditional oil and gas and renewables disputes. These matters involve disputes among operators and service companies, landowners, working interest owners and governmental authorities. These claims include environmental, reservoir, and other property and economic damages. Andrew frequently litigates disputes in Louisiana, where he is also licensed and experienced in applying Louisiana's civil law regime. Energy Infrastructure and Decommissioning. Andrew represents owners and contractors in a range of energy infrastructure and decommissioning disputes. This includes disputes involving the construction of greenfield and brownfield energy projects, as well as decommissioning projects. These matters have involved pipeline systems, offshore supply vessels, renewable diesel plants, offshore platforms and production facilities, petrochemical facilities, and crude oil refineries.
jaide: Andrew specializes in energy disputes within the oil and gas, renewables, and refining sectors, with a focus on offshore and onshore energy matters, as well as energy infrastructure and decommissioning disputes.
specialties: Litigation, Oil & Gas, Infrastructure, Renewables, Clean Tech

name: Michael Black
bio: Michael F. Black is a corporate partner in Kirkland's market-leading Investment Funds Practice Group. Michael's practice focuses on the formation, structuring and operation of private investment funds, including buyout funds, growth equity funds, search funds, debt funds, real estate funds and single asset funds and special purpose vehicles. Michael works extensively with well-established managers as well as first-time fund sponsors and emerging managers while structuring, marketing, negotiating and closing complex investment funds. Michael advises sponsors on firm-wide and fund-specific economic, governance, transactional and operational matters, including carry and fee plans, new product development, co-investments, continuation vehicles, secondaries, investment warehousing, cross fund transactions, investor relations, regulatory compliance and general corporate matters. In addition, Michael also focuses on representing private equity sponsors in connection with the organization, structuring, and operation of their management companies. Representative Clients Aria Growth Partners, Beecken Petty O'Keefe & Company, Chicago Pacific Founders, CID Capital, Eagle Merchant Partners, EW Healthcare Partners, Hidden Harbor Capital Partners, KLH Capital, Luminate Capital Partners, Mill Point Capital Partners, NexPhase Capital, O2 Investment Partners, Primus Capital, Principal Real Estate, Provenance, Shore Capital Partners, Soundcore Capital Partners, Treville Capital Management, Two Roads Partners, Tyree D'Angelo Partners, Victor Capital Partners, Victory Park Capital, Warren Equity Partners.
jaide: Michael specializes in the formation, structuring, and operation of private investment funds, including various types such as buyout funds, growth equity funds, and real estate funds.
specialties: Compliance/Regulatory, Private Equity, Investment Funds

name: Patrick Corrigan
bio: Patrick Corrigan is a corporate energy and infrastructure partner in the Houston office of Kirkland & Ellis LLP. Patrick's practice focuses on advising clients on a wide variety of commercial transactions and operations including both domestic and cross-border mergers, acquisitions and strategic investments, projects and infrastructure and energy and resources. He has extensive experience in the LNG, oil and gas, mining and power sectors having advised clients at all stages on a variety of greenfield and brownfield projects and investments in multiple jurisdictions around the globe. Patrick has also spent time as a secondee at Woodside Energy in its Shipping and LNG Marketing Team and ConocoPhillips in their general corporate team. Patrick has previously worked in both Japan and Australia and is dual qualified to practice in Texas and Australia.
specialties: M&A, Project Finance, Transactional, Infrastructure, Project Development, Oil & Gas

name: Kevin Crews
bio: Kevin is a founding partner of the Kirkland & Ellis Dallas office, joining in 2018 to help establish the new office. With nearly 20 years of legal experience, Kevin's practice focuses on significant M&A/private equity transactions with a primary emphasis on: (i) mergers and acquisitions, including auctions, carve-outs and divestitures; (ii) joint ventures, including related greenfield and project development; and (iii) equity investments, including preferred equity (debt-like and convertible/participating), structured equity (with and without back-leverage), recapitalizations and other complex equity holder arrangements. Kevin regularly advises leading private equity sponsors, including Morgan Stanley Infrastructure, CPPIB, Ontario Teachers', Tailwater, TPG, Blue Star, ORIX, Elda River, EOC, EIG, Old Ironsides, Energy Spectrum, Cresta, GPI Capital and Endurance, as well as strategic clients. Clients have described Kevin as "exceptional in every way," delivering "great market knowledge" and "highly commercial" legal advice, and "always there for you with the right answer, no matter the situation" in connection with his recognition in various national industry publications, including The Legal 500 U.S. Kevin has broad experience negotiating and closing transactions across many industries, including (without limitation) energy, infrastructure, logistics, industrials, transportation, real estate, forestry/timber, business services, consumer, apparel, manufacturing, technology, media and telecom. In the energy space, where clients have described Kevin as "an advisor in all aspects of energy investing," Kevin's practice covers traditional midstream, upstream, downstream and oilfield services, and extends to all current and emerging energy transition and climate verticals. Prior to Columbia Law School, Kevin worked in the New York-based M&A department at a leading bulge bracket investment bank.
jaide: Kevin specializes in significant M&A/private equity transactions with a primary emphasis on mergers and acquisitions, joint ventures, and equity investments across various industries, particularly in the energy sector.
specialties: M&A, Energy, Private Equity, Joint Ventures

name: Nicholas Giannuzzi
bio: Nicholas Giannuzzi is a partner in the Investment Funds Practice Group in the New York office of Kirkland & Ellis LLP. Nicholas' practice focuses on advising investment fund sponsors on a wide range of strategic and operational matters, including in connection with the formation, structuring, marketing, management and regulatory compliance of private equity and similar funds, as well as the operational, legal and regulatory issues faced by their sponsors. Nicholas has significant experience counseling both established and emerging sponsors across a wide range of investment strategies, as well as assisting sponsors in connection with negotiating and launching separately managed accounts, co-investment funds, aviation dedicated funds and other investment arrangements. His work ranges from advising established sponsors in connection with multibillion-dollar global fundraising mandates to guiding emerging managers through all aspects of establishing their operations and raising capital.
jaide: Nicholas Giannuzzi specializes in advising investment fund sponsors on a wide range of strategic and operational matters, including formation, structuring, marketing, management, and regulatory compliance of private equity and similar funds.
specialties: Compliance/Regulatory, Investment Funds, Fund Formation, Investment Management

name: Richard Grossmann
bio: Richard A. Grossmann is a partner in the Real Estate Practice Group in the New York office of Kirkland & Ellis LLP. He represents both U.S. and non-U.S. clients in complex commercial real estate transactions, including joint ventures and other equity capital arrangements, acquisitions, dispositions, financing and M&A transactions involving real estate and real estate-related businesses. Less More
specialties: P&S, Finance

name: Sophia Han
bio: Sophia Han is a tax partner in the New York office of Kirkland & Ellis LLP. Sophia's practice focuses on the tax aspects of domestic M&A and the renewable energy sector. She regularly advises project sponsors, tax equity investors and lenders on energy tax credits, project developments and financings, and M&A transactions for wind, solar and energy storage assets. Sophia represents clients on a variety of other federal income tax matters. Her experience also includes advising clients across a variety of industries, including medical device, retail and tech, on transfer pricing disputes.
specialties: M&A, Federal, Credits

name: John Kaercher
bio: John is committed to excellence." - Chambers USA, 2025 John Kaercher is a partner in Kirkland's corporate transactional group. His practice is primarily focused on counseling corporate clients, private equity sponsors and portfolio companies in structuring, negotiating and executing complex business transactions, including domestic and cross-border mergers and acquisitions, divestitures, joint ventures, leveraged buyouts, going-private transactions, purchases and sales through bankruptcy, carve-out transactions, corporate restructurings, recapitalizations, dividends and equity financings as well as general corporate matters. John has led representative matters ranging in size from several million dollars to approximately 15 billion in a variety of industries, including the technology, media/entertainment, telecommunications, CPG, energy and infrastructure sectors (including digital infrastructure, oil and gas, power, renewables and energy transition). John forms long-lasting relationships with his clients and is routinely called upon to lead complicated transactions on expedited timelines. In addition, John frequents the speaker circuits, regularly participating on panels or providing insights on topical matters.
specialties: Finance, Restructuring, Cross-Border, M&A, Joint Ventures, Private Equity

name: Thomas Laughlin
bio: Thomas Laughlin is a founding partner of Kirkland's Dallas office. He represents private equity firms, family offices, other investment firms and publicly-traded and privately-held companies in mergers and acquisitions, strategic investments, de-SPAC transactions, joint ventures, restructurings and financings. Thomas works closely with middle-market sponsors such as Trinity Hunt Partners, Braemont Capital, Maple Park Capital and Haywood Lane Partners, and regularly advises family offices on direct investments. He has also represented many of the industry's leading firms, including Allied Industrial Partners, Apollo, Ares, Blackstone, Blue Owl, Chambers Energy Capital, Clearlake Capital Group, CPPIB, Cresta, GRP Energy Capital, HBK Capital Management, HGGC, Maverick Ventures, Oaktree, Pine Brook Partners, Quantum, RedBird Capital Partners, Riverstone Holdings, Tailwater, TPG Capital, Trive Capital, Vortus Investments and Warwick Capital Partners. His practice spans a wide range of industries, with particular experience in technology and business services, consumer, healthcare, manufacturing, aviation and logistics, as well as energy and infrastructure. Thomas is also active in civic and philanthropic organizations, including the Salesmanship Club of Dallas.
jaide: Thomas specializes in representing private equity firms, family offices, investment firms, and companies in mergers and acquisitions, strategic investments, and various financial transactions across a wide range of industries.
specialties: Capital Markets, M&A, Private Equity, Joint Ventures, Restructuring, Finance

name: Becca Lock
bio: Rebecca Lock is a partner in the Investment Funds Group at Kirkland & Ellis LLP, where she focuses on structuring, negotiating and forming real estate funds and their management companies and related matters. Rebecca has represented numerous global real estate and other private equity fund sponsor in establishing open-end and closed-end investment funds, vehicles and other accounts, including programmatic joint ventures and co-investment deals, with a broad range of investment strategies. Rebecca has been involved as legal counsel in a wide range of complex matters in the PE space, including extraordinary events involving real estate funds and their sponsors, such as high-stakes "key person" events, strategic investments in a fund sponsor and minority stake sales, seed capital and anchor investor arrangements and other significant matters and transactions. Less More
specialties: Private Equity, Joint Ventures, Investment Management, REITs, Investment Funds

name: Nadia Murad
bio: Nadia Murad is a partner in the Investment Funds Group at Kirkland & Ellis LLP where she advises leading private equity sponsors and investment managers on fundraising, platform growth, investor relations and strategic initiatives. With over 20 years of experience at the Firm advising on aggregate capital raised totaling over 100 billion, she is a trusted advisor to sponsors as they build, scale and institutionalize their investment platforms. Nadia's practice spans the full lifecycle of an investment firm, from first time fund launches to the development of global, multiproduct platforms. As an instrumental part of the Firm's leading global IFG platform, she advises clients on a wide variety of fund formation and related matters, including numerous mega-fund formations and fundraises across a broad spectrum of strategies, including private equity buyout and mega funds, hybrid (PE/hedge) and open-ended funds, first-time funds, secondary funds, continuation funds, co-invest vehicles, feeder vehicles and HNW retail platforms, fund-of-one and single asset vehicles and alternative investment vehicle structures. She regularly helps clients navigate ongoing regulatory and compliance matters, conflicts, allocations, cross-fund transactions and evolving investor expectations. In addition to representing clients on fund formation matters, Nadia is frequently called upon at critical inflection points to advise clients on a wide range of complex business transactions and operational matters, including but not limited to: acquisitions and structuring, strategic expansions and new product launches, AUM growth, current and anticipated trends and market developments, investor communications and investor relations considerations, establishment of new offices, and liquidity solutions including structured secondaries and GP-led transactions. Nadia also has been a key partner representing private equity sponsors on internal firm structuring and restructuring, including complex succession planning, team expansions and separations, economic arrangements and founder-related investments. Nadia has been instrumental in establishing Kirkland's family office practice. She regularly represents family offices and high net worth individuals in connection with private equity investments, co-investments and sports-related investments. Nadia is recognized as a leading global Entertainment, Sports and Media lawyer for Investment Funds, and is a recognized thought leader in the private funds and asset management space, frequently speaking and moderating at industry conferences and authoring widely cited publications on fund structuring, ESG, regulatory developments and market trends. Nadia has been quoted numerous times by Law360, The Deal, Financial Worldwide, Private Equity Law Report and authored numerous Kirkland thought-leadership pieces. Within the Firm, Nadia plays an active leadership role and has been integral in leading strategic initiatives across the Investment Funds platform, collaborating across practices to deliver integrated client solutions, mentoring and training junior partners and associates, and contributing to recruiting, client development, investor solutions and AI strategy, as well as pro bono contributions. With a personalized, relationship-driven approach to her practice and a passion for the private equity asset class, Nadia has an unwavering commitment to providing clients with best-in-class service, commercial advice, innovative solutions and strategic support at every stage of their firm's lifecycle. Nadia is an active supporter of the arts and is a member of the Women's Board of the Museum of Contemporary Art in Chicago and the Whitney Contemporaries. Representative Clients 3G Capital, Inc., Arctos Partners, Ares Management, Centerview Partners, Clearlake Capital Group, Crestview Partners, Dawson Partners, Golden Gate Capital, GTCR, ICONIQ Capital, Irving Place Capital, Northern Trust, Patria Finance Limited, Warburg Pincus, Wynnchurch Capital, York Capital Management
jaide: Specializes in advising private equity sponsors and investment managers on fund formation, fundraising, platform growth, regulatory compliance and related transactional matters, including GP-led and secondary solutions.
specialties: Finance, Compliance/Regulatory, Restructuring, M&A, Private Equity, Investment Management, Investment Funds, Fund Formation

name: Byron Pacheco
bio: Byron Pacheco is a litigation partner in Kirkland's New York office. His practice focuses on complex commercial disputes, both at the trial court level and on appeal, in a variety of substantive areas, including contract disputes, securities class actions, consumer class actions, corporate governance, founder disputes, fraud, products liability, labor and employment, and securities matters. Byron also counsels public companies and private equity clients on transaction-related litigation risks, providing corporate governance advice and strategic litigation advice on a variety of issues. His clients have included, Abbott Labs, Blackstone, Charlesbank, FirstFire Capital, Harvest Partners, Humana, JUUL Labs, KKR & Co., Lee Enterprises, Melody Capital, Owl Rock Capital Group, Sentinel Capital Partners, Thoma Bravo, Urban Compass, Warburg Pincus and Welspun. Less More
specialties: Class Action, Investigations, Government, Securities, Antitrust, Labor & Employment

name: Jennifer Wu
bio: Jennifer Wu is a capital markets partner in the Austin office of Kirkland & Ellis LLP. She counsels companies across the technology and energy industries in capital raises and public offerings, including initial public offerings (IPOs), follow-on offerings and high yield debt offerings. In addition, she advises her clients on federal securities law compliance, ongoing corporate governance, and Exchange Act reporting and disclosure matters. She is listed as "Up and Coming" for Capital Markets: Debt & Equity in Texas and Central US – Nationwide by Chambers USA and Chambers Global, recognized for Capital Markets: Equity Offerings by The Legal 500 United States and as a "Rising Star" by Texas Super Lawyers (2019–2022). Jennifer has also served on Law360's Capital Markets and Texas Editorial Advisory Boards. She is a frequent presenter and go-to resource on the topics of board service, corporate governance, and IPO and public company readiness. Jennifer is an active leader in the Austin community and has served on the Boards of Directors of The Junior League of Austin and United Way for Greater Austin.
jaide: Jennifer specializes in capital markets, specifically advising companies in the technology and energy industries on capital raises, public offerings, federal securities law compliance, and corporate governance matters.
specialties: Energy, Compliance/Regulatory, Securities, Public Company, Capital Markets, IPOs

name: Alexandra Hill
bio: Alex focuses her practice on many aspects of commercial real estate and investment. She has extensive experience representing owners, developers, investors and lenders in the origination, acquisition and disposition (including securitization and syndication) of mortgage loans, mezzanine financings and subordinate debt, post-closing modifications and loan sales and purchases involving various asset classes including multifamily, industrial, retail, self-storage, office and hospitality properties. In addition, Alex has significant experience with C-PACE financing, construction financing and ground lease transactions. Prior to joining the firm, Alex worked in the Philadelphia office of a large international law firm in their global finance practice.
specialties: Finance, Leasing, P&S, Condo Associations, Hospitality, Construction, Real Estate, Mezzanine Finance

name: Katherine Missimer
bio: Kate concentrates her practice on representing property owners and developers with respect to real estate development projects, including land use and zoning relief, as well as the preparation of condominium and planned community documentation. She has experience representing clients before the Philadelphia Zoning Board of Adjustment and the zoning hearing boards of surrounding townships and municipalities as well as facilitating discussions between developers and other governmental departments and agencies. Additionally, Kate represents new and emerging companies, nonprofits and for-profit corporations with respect to entity formation, contract drafting and negotiation, asset purchases and sales, mergers, acquisitions, regulatory compliance, private equity and venture capital funding, contracts for employment, management and consulting and joint ventures. Kate takes great pride in representing her clients' interests to the fullest, whether advocating for approval of a development project before the community and the zoning board or negotiating the most favorable terms possible for the sale of a corporate client's assets. She is driven, dedicated, hardworking, and responsive.
specialties: P&S, Development, Land Use, Zoning

name: Christopher Terry
bio: Chris Terry advises clients in the areas of real estate and real estate finance law, including joint venture transactions, real estate development, sales and acquisitions, financing, leasing and real estate private equity. He has particular experience in medical office and healthcare development, commercial leasing, and the sale and acquisition of hotels, resorts, and commercial office buildings. Chris has advised a diverse group of clients, including owners, operators, developers, lenders, borrowers, landlords, tenants and investors in sophisticated transactions in and across the continental United States and Hawaii. Chris is a member of the inaugural Rising Leaders Council of Revista. In his role on the RLC, Chris, alongside other selected leaders across the healthcare real estate industry, acts as a resource to the Revista Advisory Board and Executive Team. As the sole legal advisor on the council, Chris offers a strong and diverse perspective as it relates to investments, best practices and industry trends. Chris was named a "Rising Star" by Real Estate Weekly in 2019.
specialties: Development, Finance, Leasing, P&S, Landlord/Tenant

name: David Endersbee
bio: David Endersbee primarily advises foreign and domestic airlines, foreign governments, companies, airports, and manufacturers on a variety of operational, commercial, antitrust, and regulatory matters relating to U.S. aviation policy, regulation, and enforcement. These matters include operating authorizations, international air service agreements, and Department of Transportation, Federal Aviation Administration, Transportation Security Administration, and Customs and Border Protection regulations and policies. Mr. Endersbee was selected for inclusion in Who's Who Legal: Transport 2022 and 2023, Aviation Regulatory chapter. Mr. Endersbee is the Exclusive IR Global Advisor for Aviation Law in Washington, DC.
specialties: Compliance/Regulatory, Aviation, FAA

name: Kimberly Miller
bio: Dr. Kim Miller serves as a single point of contact for all IP-related matters her clients face. For more established companies, she easily adapts to their processes and procedures, blending seamlessly into their existing corporate structure while adding valuable insight regarding how to better leverage their IP assets. For smaller, emerging and fast-growth companies, Kim works diligently to identify the vision going forward, as well as to provide advice and procedural solutions that fit their agenda today and into the future. Regardless of the size or extent of her clients' IP assets, Kim is totally dedicated to conserving financial and other resources while seeking to ensure they get what they specifically need to succeed. A past chair of the firm's International Department, Kim has conducted patent prosecution in more than 45 jurisdictions throughout the world. She offers a diverse practice in IP portfolio management and development, due diligence and landscape analysis, as well as licensing initiatives and obtaining design patents. Kim's clients include individual inventors, venture funded start-ups, university spin-offs and publicly traded companies. Her practice spans a variety of technical disciplines, including chemistry, pharmaceuticals, polymers and biotechnology. Kim is recognized in The Best Lawyers in America, listed as a "World IP Leader" by World Intellectual Property Review by WIPR, a "Life Science Star" by Legal Media Group and in the "World's Leading Patent Professionals" in the Patent 1000 guide by Intellectual Asset Management, The Recorder's "Women Leaders in Tech Law," The Legal 500 as an outstanding practitioner, and San Diego Business Journal's "Best of the Bar," Kim is known and appreciated for treating her clients like family, understanding the importance of their business goals and remaining conscientiously respectful of their time. Kim is the ultimate problem-solver who is totally focused on analyzing and orchestrating all the moving parts of her client's short- and long-term goals, creating a path for them that is actionable and effectual. Kim's notable successes include her representation of Alios BioPharma, Inc., which led to a 1.75 billion deal with Johnson & Johnson.
